diffs.patch:
Index: .tmux.conf
===================================================================
--- .tmux.conf	
+++ .tmux.conf	
@@ -1,462 +1,464 @@
 # Basic tmux configuration
 set -g default-terminal "tmux-256color"
 set -ga terminal-overrides ",*:Tc:RGB,xterm-256color:Ms=\\E]52;c;%p2%s\\7,tmux-256color:Ms=\\E]52;c;%p2%s\\7"
 
 # Set prefix to Ctrl-s (more ergonomic than Ctrl-Space)
 set -g prefix C-s
 unbind C-b
 unbind C-a
 # Unbind C-z to ensure it's passed through to nested applications (like Neovim terminal)
 unbind C-z
 # Double tap Ctrl-s to send literal Ctrl-s to applications
 bind-key C-s send-prefix
 
 # Enable key repeat
 set -g repeat-time 0
 
 # Basic settings
 set -g mouse on
 set -g escape-time 0
 set -g focus-events on
 set -g history-limit 100000
 set -g base-index 1
 setw -g pane-base-index 1
 set -g renumber-windows on
 setw -g automatic-rename off
 
 # Prevent Claude Code nested-session false positives in tmux
 set-environment -gr CLAUDECODE
 
 # When a session is destroyed, switch to the most recently active session
 # instead of detaching the client (which would close the terminal).
 # Tested with tmux 3.5a.
 set -g detach-on-destroy off
 
 # Proactively ensure "main" session exists at server start.
 # Combined with the session-closed hook below, this guarantees a fallback
 # target is always available for detach-on-destroy.
 # exit-empty is set to off so the server survives the brief window between
 # sole-session destruction and hook recreation.
 set -g exit-empty off
 run-shell 'tmux has-session -t main 2>/dev/null || tmux new-session -d -s main'
 
 # Reactively recreate "main" if destroyed while the server is still running.
 set-hook -g session-closed 'if-shell "! tmux has-session -t main 2>/dev/null" "new-session -d -s main"'
 
 # Activity monitoring - custom completion detection
 # Monitors activity, then checks for completion after silence period
 setw -g monitor-activity on
 set -g visual-activity off  # Don't show visual alerts
 set -g activity-action any  # Trigger on ALL windows including current (enables Claude highlighting)
 # Disable visual styling for windows with activity (no grey background)
 set -g window-status-activity-style "fg=#6c7086,bg=#1e1e2e"
 
 # Disable silence monitoring (alert-silence hook doesn't fire reliably)
 setw -g monitor-silence 0
 set -g visual-silence off
 
 # Agent activity monitoring:
 #   Claude: event-driven via scripts/bin/claude wrapper (injects --settings hooks)
 #   Codex/Opencode: polling fallback via tmux-claude-watcher.sh daemon (10s interval)
 # Explicitly unset stale hooks from earlier config versions
 set-hook -gu alert-activity
 set-hook -gu after-select-window
 
 # Start watcher daemon for codex/opencode (claude is handled by the wrapper)
 run-shell '~/dotfiles/scripts/tmux/tmux-claude-watcher.sh start >/dev/null 2>&1 || true'
 
 # Bell on activity completion - disabled
 set -g bell-action none
 set -g visual-bell off  # Disable visual bell flash
 
 # Clipboard integration
 set -g set-clipboard on
 # Allow OSC-52 escape sequences to pass through to the terminal
 # Required for Neovim clipboard to work in SSH/container sessions
 set -g allow-passthrough on
 
 # OS-specific default shell configuration
 # reattach-to-user-namespace removed: not needed since macOS 12 (Monterey)
 # pbcopy/pbpaste and OSC-52 work natively in tmux on modern macOS
 # NOTE: default-command is set here to block tmux-sensible from overriding it
 # with "reattach-to-user-namespace -l $SHELL" (which spawns zsh, not fish).
 # After TPM loads, default-command is reset to empty (see post-TPM section)
 # so tmux launches default-shell directly without nesting.
 if-shell "uname | grep -q Darwin" \
   "set -g default-shell /opt/homebrew/bin/fish; \
    set -g default-command /opt/homebrew/bin/fish" \
   "set -g default-shell /usr/bin/fish; \
    set -g default-command /usr/bin/fish"
 
 # Enhanced Copy Mode Configuration
 # Use vi-style keys in copy mode
 setw -g mode-keys vi
 
 # Easy copy mode entry with prefix
 # Use v with prefix (Ctrl-s v) instead of [
 bind-key v copy-mode
 # Keep the default [ binding as well
 bind-key [ copy-mode
 
 # Copy mode key bindings (vi-style)
 bind-key -T copy-mode-vi v send-keys -X begin-selection
 bind-key -T copy-mode-vi C-v send-keys -X rectangle-toggle
 # Copy to the system clipboard and exit copy mode, matching Vim yank expectations.
 # OS-specific clipboard command: pbcopy on macOS, xclip on Linux
 if-shell "uname | grep -q Darwin" \
   "bind-key -T copy-mode-vi y send-keys -X copy-pipe-and-cancel 'pbcopy'; \
    bind-key -T copy-mode-vi MouseDragEnd1Pane send-keys -X copy-pipe-and-cancel 'pbcopy'; \
    bind-key -T copy-mode-vi Enter send-keys -X copy-pipe-and-cancel 'pbcopy'" \
   "bind-key -T copy-mode-vi y send-keys -X copy-pipe-and-cancel 'xclip -selection clipboard'; \
    bind-key -T copy-mode-vi MouseDragEnd1Pane send-keys -X copy-pipe-and-cancel 'xclip -selection clipboard'; \
    bind-key -T copy-mode-vi Enter send-keys -X copy-pipe-and-cancel 'xclip -selection clipboard'"
 
 # Clean copy: Y joins lines (fixes copy-paste line breaks from TUI apps like Claude Code)
 # Regular y = raw copy, Y = join lines (for commands), Alt-y = smart (preserve paragraphs)
 # Note: C-y is NOT used here as it conflicts with vi copy-mode scroll-up
 # Note: M-y requires terminal Alt/Meta key support (works in Ghostty/WezTerm/iTerm2).
 #   Y (Shift-y) is the primary binding and works in all terminals.
 # Note: tmux smart mode is a lightweight paragraph-joiner. For full-featured mode
 #   with list/indent/code-fence preservation, use Fish: cfx -p
 if-shell "uname | grep -q Darwin" \
   "bind-key -T copy-mode-vi Y send-keys -X copy-pipe-and-cancel '$HOME/dotfiles/scripts/tmux/tmux-copy-cleanup.sh join'; \
    bind-key -T copy-mode-vi M-y send-keys -X copy-pipe-and-cancel '$HOME/dotfiles/scripts/tmux/tmux-copy-cleanup.sh smart'" \
   "bind-key -T copy-mode-vi Y send-keys -X copy-pipe-and-cancel '$HOME/dotfiles/scripts/tmux/tmux-copy-cleanup.sh join'; \
    bind-key -T copy-mode-vi M-y send-keys -X copy-pipe-and-cancel '$HOME/dotfiles/scripts/tmux/tmux-copy-cleanup.sh smart'"
 
 # Additional convenient bindings for copy mode
 bind-key -T copy-mode-vi Space send-keys -X begin-selection
 bind-key -T copy-mode-vi Escape send-keys -X cancel
 # Match Neovim scroll convention: C-d scrolls up, C-f scrolls down.
 bind-key -T copy-mode-vi C-d send-keys -X halfpage-up
 bind-key -T copy-mode-vi C-f send-keys -X halfpage-down
 bind-key -T copy-mode-vi C-y send-keys -X scroll-up
 bind-key -T copy-mode-vi C-e send-keys -X scroll-down
 # Double-click to select word, triple-click to select line
 bind-key -T copy-mode-vi DoubleClick1Pane select-pane \; send-keys -X select-word
 bind-key -T copy-mode-vi TripleClick1Pane select-pane \; send-keys -X select-line
 
 # Search bindings in copy mode
 bind-key -T copy-mode-vi / command-prompt -i -p "search down" "send -X search-forward-incremental \"%%%\""
 bind-key -T copy-mode-vi ? command-prompt -i -p "search up" "send -X search-backward-incremental \"%%%\""
 
 # Key bindings
 # NOTE: Context-aware 'r' key binding is defined AFTER TPM loads (see bottom of file)
 # to override tmux-powerkit's reload binding
 
 # Window management
 bind c new-window -c "#{pane_current_path}"
 bind % split-window -h -c "#{pane_current_path}"
 bind '"' split-window -v -c "#{pane_current_path}"
 # Pane navigation handled by vim-tmux-navigator (Ctrl-h/j/k/l)
 # Keeping prefix+h/j/k/l as fallback when vim-tmux-navigator is not active
 # bind h select-pane -L
 # bind j select-pane -D
 # bind k select-pane -U
 # bind l select-pane -R
 
 # Smart adaptive splitting - automatically chooses horizontal or vertical based on pane dimensions
 # Uses native tmux format arithmetic (no shell spawn): 8*width vs 20*height accounts for ~2:1 char aspect ratio
 bind -r Space if-shell -F "#{e|<:#{e|*:8,#{pane_width}},#{e|*:20,#{pane_height}}}" \
   "splitw -v -c '#{pane_current_path}' ; display '#[fg=#a6e3a1]↔ Split Horizontal#[default]'" \
   "splitw -h -c '#{pane_current_path}' ; display '#[fg=#89dceb]↕ Split Vertical#[default]'"
 
 # Respawn pane keybinding (restart dead/frozen process)
 bind R respawn-pane -k \; display '#[fg=#a6e3a1] Pane Respawned#[default]'
 
 # M and C-m bindings removed — watcher colors are purely realtime, no manual clear needed
 
 # Toggle logging - save pane output to a file (press again to stop)
 bind L pipe-pane -o "cat >> ~/dotfiles/tmux-logs/\$(date +%Y%m%d-%H%M)-#W.log" \; \
   display '#[fg=#e0af68]📝 Logging: ~/dotfiles/tmux-logs/\$(date +%Y%m%d-%H%M)-#W.log#[default]'
 
 # List of plugins
 set -g @plugin 'tmux-plugins/tpm'
 set -g @plugin 'tmux-plugins/tmux-sensible'
 set -g @plugin 'tmux-plugins/tmux-yank'
 set -g @plugin 'tmux-plugins/tmux-prefix-highlight'
 # set -g @plugin 'alexwforsythe/tmux-which-key'  # Disabled - conflicts with Space key binding
 set -g @plugin 'sainnhe/tmux-fzf'
 set -g @plugin 'tmux-plugins/tmux-open'
 # Configure tmux-open
 set -g @open-S 'https://www.google.com/search?q='
 # Use 'o' to open URLs/files in copy mode
 set -g @open 'o'
 # Use Ctrl-o to open in editor
 set -g @open-editor 'C-o'
 # tmux-copycat removed: deprecated, slow regex on scrollback. Use native / search in copy mode
 set -g @plugin 'tmux-plugins/tmux-pain-control'
 # vim-tmux-navigator: seamless navigation between vim and tmux
 set -g @plugin 'christoomey/vim-tmux-navigator'
 set -g @plugin 'tmux-plugins/tmux-sidebar'
 # tmux-thumbs - fast Rust-based text hints (replacement for tmux-fingers)
 set -g @plugin 'fcsonline/tmux-thumbs'
 set -g @thumbs-key F
 # OS-specific clipboard command for tmux-thumbs
 if-shell "uname | grep -q Darwin" \
   "set -g @thumbs-command 'echo -n {} | pbcopy'" \
   "set -g @thumbs-command 'echo -n {} | xclip -selection clipboard'"
 set -g @thumbs-upcase-command 'open {}'  # Shift+hint opens URL/path
 set -g @thumbs-hint-fg-color yellow
 set -g @thumbs-hint-bg-color black
 set -g @thumbs-select-fg-color yellow
 set -g @thumbs-select-bg-color black
 
 # extrakto - extract text from pane and insert (paths, URLs, IPs, git hashes, etc.)
 # Access with: Ctrl-s + t (text extraction) - overrides default clock-mode
 set -g @plugin 'laktak/extrakto'
 set -g @extrakto_key 't'
 set -g @extrakto_split_size '15'
 set -g @extrakto_popup_size '80%'
 set -g @extrakto_grab_area 'recent'
 # OS-specific clipboard for extrakto
 if-shell "uname | grep -q Darwin" \
   "set -g @extrakto_clip_tool 'pbcopy'; set -g @extrakto_fzf_tool '/opt/homebrew/bin/fzf'" \
   "set -g @extrakto_clip_tool 'xclip -selection clipboard'; set -g @extrakto_fzf_tool 'fzf'"
 
 # tmux-fzf configuration - unified FZF menu for tmux operations
 # Access with: Ctrl-s + f (Find/FZF menu)
 TMUX_FZF_LAUNCH_KEY="f"
 TMUX_FZF_OPTIONS="-p -w 80% -h 80% -m"
 TMUX_FZF_ORDER="session|window|pane|command|keybinding|clipboard"
 
 # tmux-notify - macOS Notification Center alerts when commands finish
 # Access with: Ctrl-s + m (monitor toggle)
 # Works alongside custom tmux-activity-monitor.sh for dual notification
 set -g @plugin 'rickstaa/tmux-notify'
 set -g @tnotify-verbose 'on'
 set -g @tnotify-sleep-duration '5'
 
 # tmux-1password - quick access to 1Password vault items
 # Access with: Ctrl-s + p (password)
 # Requires: brew install --cask 1password-cli && op signin
 set -g @plugin 'yardnsm/tmux-1password'
 set -g @1password-key 'p'
 set -g @1password-copy-to-clipboard 'on'
 set -g @1password-vault ''
 
 # tmux-fuzzback - FZF-powered scrollback buffer search
 # Access with: Ctrl-s + b (buffer search)
 set -g @plugin 'roosta/tmux-fuzzback'
 set -g @fuzzback-bind 'b'
 set -g @fuzzback-popup 1
 set -g @fuzzback-popup-size '80%'
 
 # tmux session persistence across server/device restarts
 set -g @plugin 'tmux-plugins/tmux-resurrect'
 set -g @resurrect-processes 'nvim vim "~ocv->ocv"'
 set -g @resurrect-strategy-nvim 'session'
 set -g @resurrect-strategy-vim 'session'
 set -g @resurrect-capture-pane-contents 'on'
 # Claude Code is intentionally excluded here.
 # OpenCode is restored through the local ocv wrapper.
 
 # tmux-battery removed - was loaded but never displayed (OPTIMIZED)
 set -g @plugin 'tmux-plugins/tmux-cpu'
 # tmux-smooth-scroll removed: every scroll tick spawns bash→bash→perl chain
 # Native tmux scrolling is instant and sufficient
 
 # tmux-powerkit - Ultimate status bar framework with 42 plugins
 set -g @plugin 'fabioluciano/tmux-powerkit'
 set -gu status-right
 
 # Keep tmux-continuum last: it hooks into status-right for autosave.
 set -g @plugin 'tmux-plugins/tmux-continuum'
 set -g @continuum-restore 'on'
 set -g @continuum-save-interval '15'
 
 # tmux-powerkit configuration
 # Two-section layout: ① Session+Windows (left, seamless) | ② Plugins (right)
 
 # Section order and layout
 # 3-element order: session (left) | windows (center) | plugins (right)
 set -g @powerkit_status_order "session,windows,plugins"
 
 # Select plugins to display (comma-separated list)
 # Available: datetime, battery, cpu, memory, disk, load, temperature, fan, gpu, io, brightness, uptime, hostname,
 #           bandwidth, wifi, vpn, latency, public_ip, ssh, weather, volume, now_playing, git, github, gitlab,
 #           bitbucket, jira, kubernetes, terraform, cloud, packages, pomodoro, bitwarden, crypto, stocks, env
 # Default selection: essential system metrics and time
 set -g @powerkit_plugins 'git,datetime'
 
 # Position status bar at bottom (user preference)
 set -g status-position bottom
 
 # Theme configuration - Custom Tokyo Night Storm (editable)
 # To revert to built-in: set theme "tokyo-night" and variant "storm"
 # Edit colors in: scripts/tmux/tokyo-night-custom.sh
 set -g @powerkit_theme "custom"
 set -g @powerkit_custom_theme_path "$HOME/dotfiles/scripts/tmux/tokyo-night-custom.sh"
 
-# Separator style - ROUNDED for pill-shaped segments like in the reference image
-set -g @powerkit_separator_style "rounded"
+# Separator style - straight powerline edges, no rounded pills
+set -g @powerkit_separator_style "normal"
+set -g @powerkit_edge_separator_style "normal"
+set -g @powerkit_initial_separator_style "normal"
 
 # Spacing configuration - CRITICAL FIX for gap issue
 set -g @powerkit_elements_spacing "false"     # Remove gaps between sections
 
 # Appearance
 set -g @powerkit_transparent true             # Transparent backgrounds for panes/popup where possible
 set -g @powerkit_update_interval 15           # Git branch + datetime don't need frequent refresh
 
 # Plugin-specific configurations
 set -g @powerkit_datetime_format "%H:%M %a %d %b"  # Time and date format
 set -g @powerkit_git_show_branch true         # Show current Git branch
 set -g @powerkit_git_show_status true         # Show Git status indicators (*, +, etc.)
 set -g @powerkit_bandwidth_interface "auto"   # Auto-detect network interface
 set -g @powerkit_wifi_show_signal true        # Show WiFi signal strength
 
 # Catppuccin Mocha Theme with omerxx-inspired aesthetics
 
 # Session Management - Single unified manager with tmuxinator integration
 # Access with: Ctrl-s + S
 bind S display-popup -E -h 80% -w 80% "$HOME/dotfiles/scripts/tmux/tmux-session-manager.sh"
 
 # Session Trash Recovery - Browse and restore killed sessions
 # Access with: Ctrl-s + T
 bind T display-popup -E -h 80% -w 80% "$HOME/dotfiles/scripts/tmux/tmux-session-trash.sh restore"
 
 # Which-key configuration (disabled - conflicts with Space binding)
 # set -g @which-key-popup 1
 
 # =============================================================================
 # STATUS BAR CONFIGURATION - Three Section Layout
 # =============================================================================
 # ① Session (left) | ② Plugins (center) | ③ Windows (right/center)
-# Powered by tmux-powerkit with rounded, pill-shaped segments
+# Powered by tmux-powerkit with straight-edged segments
 # =============================================================================
 
 # Status bar layout configuration
 set -g status-justify centre              # Center window list
 set -g status-left-length 50              # Space for session indicator
 set -g status-right-length 150            # Space for plugin pills
 
 # Background styling (powerkit themes will override with their colors)
 set -g status-style "bg=#1e1e2e,fg=#cdd6f4"
 
 # Ensure pane backgrounds are not forced to a solid color
 # (lets terminal/wezterm transparency show through and enables nvim winblend)
 set -g window-style 'bg=default'
 set -g window-active-style 'bg=default'
 set -g popup-style 'bg=default,fg=default'
 
 # Window status styling (powerkit may override, but provides fallback)
-# Active window - highlighted with rounded background
-setw -g window-status-current-style "fg=#1e1e2e,bg=#89b4fa,bold"
+# Active window - highlighted fallback when powerkit is unavailable
+setw -g window-status-current-style "fg=#1e1e2e,bg=#73daca,bold"
 # Inactive windows - subdued appearance
 setw -g window-status-style "fg=#6c7086,bg=#313244"
 
 # Pane borders - subtle with accent on active
 set -g pane-border-style "fg=#313244"
 set -g pane-active-border-style "fg=#cba6f7"
 
 # Message style
 set -g message-style "bg=#313244,fg=#cdd6f4"
 set -g message-command-style "bg=#313244,fg=#cdd6f4"
 
 # Copy mode colors
 set -g mode-style "bg=#45475a,fg=#cdd6f4"
 
 # # Update status bar every 30 seconds for CPU/RAM (OPTIMIZED: reduced from 10s)
 # # This reduces refresh overhead by 3x while still providing timely updates
 # set -g status-interval 30
 
 # Auto-install TPM if not present
 if "test ! -d ~/.tmux/plugins/tpm" \
    "run 'git clone https://github.com/tmux-plugins/tpm ~/.tmux/plugins/tpm && ~/.tmux/plugins/tpm/bin/install_plugins'"
 
 # Automatically split new windows and run claude (DISABLED)
 # This creates a vertical split with claude in the right pane for every new window
 # set-hook -g after-new-window 'split-window -h -p 40 "claude"'
 
 # Manual keybinding to open Claude Code when needed
 # Prefix + C opens Claude in a vertical split (40% width on the right)
 bind C split-window -h -p 40 "claude --dangerously-skip-permissions"
 
 # Quick launch: Ctrl-s a opens Claude with smart adaptive split
 bind a if-shell -F "#{e|<:#{e|*:8,#{pane_width}},#{e|*:20,#{pane_height}}}" \
   "splitw -v -c '#{pane_current_path}' 'claude --dangerously-skip-permissions --effort max --remote-control' ; display '#[fg=#a6e3a1]↔ Split Horizontal (Claude)#[default]'" \
   "splitw -h -c '#{pane_current_path}' 'claude --dangerously-skip-permissions --effort max --remote-control' ; display '#[fg=#89dceb]↕ Split Vertical (Claude)#[default]'"
 
 # Quick launch: Ctrl-s O opens OpenCode in its allow-permissions setup
 bind O if-shell -F "#{e|<:#{e|*:8,#{pane_width}},#{e|*:20,#{pane_height}}}" \
   "splitw -v -c '#{pane_current_path}' '$HOME/dotfiles/scripts/opencode/tmux-open.sh' ; display '#[fg=#a6e3a1]↔ Split Horizontal (OpenCode)#[default]'" \
   "splitw -h -c '#{pane_current_path}' '$HOME/dotfiles/scripts/opencode/tmux-open.sh' ; display '#[fg=#89dceb]↕ Split Vertical (OpenCode)#[default]'"
 
 # Quick launch: Ctrl-s e opens Neovim in current directory
 # C-c first to normalize Fish vi-mode state (lands us in insert mode at empty
 # prompt) so the typed `nvim .` isn't eaten as vi-mode normal commands.
 bind e send-keys C-c "nvim ." Enter
 
 # Quick launch: Ctrl-s E opens per-directory gwtt-prompt.local.md with smart adaptive split
 bind E if-shell -F "#{e|<:#{e|*:8,#{pane_width}},#{e|*:20,#{pane_height}}}" \
   "splitw -v -c '#{pane_current_path}' '$HOME/dotfiles/scripts/tmux/gwtt-prompt-edit.sh' ; display '#[fg=#a6e3a1]↔ Split Horizontal (gwtt-prompt)#[default]'" \
   "splitw -h -c '#{pane_current_path}' '$HOME/dotfiles/scripts/tmux/gwtt-prompt-edit.sh' ; display '#[fg=#89dceb]↕ Split Vertical (gwtt-prompt)#[default]'"
 
 # URL handler binding (smart: tries visible pane first, falls back to full scrollback)
 bind-key o run-shell -b "$HOME/dotfiles/scripts/tmux/tmux-url-handler.sh"
 
 # Keep default tmux bindings for navigation
 # Prefix + O: select previous pane
 # Prefix + p: previous window (default tmux behavior)
 
 # Initialize TMUX plugin manager (keep this line at the very bottom of tmux.conf)
 run '~/.tmux/plugins/tpm/tpm'
 
 # Force tmux-open bindings after all plugins load
 run-shell '~/.tmux/plugins/tmux-open/open.tmux'
 
 # Restore prefix+x to kill-pane (extrakto plugin overrides this even with @extrakto_key changed)
 # Enhanced: capture window info before killing for worktree auto-cleanup.
 # Run the wrapper in tmux background so pane close stays responsive.
 bind-key x run-shell -b '~/dotfiles/scripts/tmux/tmux-kill-pane-cleanup.sh "#{session_name}" "#{window_name}" "#{window_panes}"'
 
 # Kill window: prefix + X (Shift-x) - closes entire window with worktree cleanup
 bind-key X confirm-before -p 'Kill window "#{window_name}"? (y/n)' \
   "run-shell -b '~/dotfiles/scripts/tmux/tmux-worktree-cleanup.sh \"#{session_name}\" \"#{window_name}\"'; kill-window"
 
 # Force status bar position and layout (override plugin defaults)
 set -g status-position bottom
 set -g status-justify centre
 
 # Final overrides to keep transparent panes/popups after plugins load
 set -g window-style 'bg=default'
 set -g window-active-style 'bg=default'
 set -g popup-style 'bg=default,fg=default'
 
 # Override powerkit's default 5s status-interval (must be after TPM)
 set -g status-interval 15
 
 # Fix nested fish: reset default-command to empty after TPM loads.
 # tmux-sensible (loaded by TPM) overrides empty default-command with
 # "reattach-to-user-namespace -l $SHELL". By setting it to fish BEFORE TPM,
 # sensible sees non-empty value and leaves it alone. Now we reset to empty
 # so tmux launches default-shell directly (no "fish -c fish" nesting).
 set -g default-command ''
 
 # Reassert clipboard copy-mode bindings after TPM plugins load.
 # tmux-yank can override these with legacy reattach-to-user-namespace commands.
 if-shell "uname | grep -q Darwin" \
   "bind-key -T copy-mode-vi y send-keys -X copy-pipe-and-cancel 'pbcopy'; \
    bind-key -T copy-mode-vi MouseDragEnd1Pane send-keys -X copy-pipe-and-cancel 'pbcopy'; \
    bind-key -T copy-mode-vi Enter send-keys -X copy-pipe-and-cancel 'pbcopy'" \
   "bind-key -T copy-mode-vi y send-keys -X copy-pipe-and-cancel 'xclip -selection clipboard'; \
    bind-key -T copy-mode-vi MouseDragEnd1Pane send-keys -X copy-pipe-and-cancel 'xclip -selection clipboard'; \
    bind-key -T copy-mode-vi Enter send-keys -X copy-pipe-and-cancel 'xclip -selection clipboard'"
 
 # Inject @wname_style into status bar window tabs (must be after TPM/powerkit)
 # Makes agent state colors visible in the status bar, not just choose-tree
 run-shell '~/dotfiles/scripts/tmux/inject-wname-style.sh'
 
 # Colored choose-tree session/window picker (must be after TPM)
 # Tree labels (session/window names, markers) use pane fg — set via select-pane -P
 # pane-mode-changed hook resets pane style when exiting choose-tree
 # Agent colors set by watcher via @wname_style: running=green, idle=yellow, stuck=red
 set-hook -g pane-mode-changed[100] 'if -F "#{pane_in_mode}" "" "select-pane -P default"'
 bind s select-pane -P 'fg=#9ece6a' \; choose-tree -Zw -F '#{?pane_format,#[fg=#7dcfff]#{pane_current_command} #[fg=#565f89](#{pane_current_path}),#{?window_format,#{@wname_style}#{window_name}#{?window_active, *,},#[fg=#565f89]#{session_windows} windows#{?session_attached, #[fg=#e0af68](attached),}}}'
 bind w select-pane -P 'fg=#9ece6a' \; choose-tree -Zw -F '#{?pane_format,#[fg=#7dcfff]#{pane_current_command} #[fg=#565f89](#{pane_current_path}),#{?window_format,#{@wname_style}#{window_name}#{?window_active, *,},#[fg=#565f89]#{session_windows} windows#{?session_attached, #[fg=#e0af68](attached),}}}'
 
 # Context-aware r key: recall when in Claude, config reload otherwise
 # Must be after TPM to override tmux-powerkit's reload binding
 bind r if-shell "ps -o args= -t #{pane_tty} 2>/dev/null | grep -q 'claude'" \
     "confirm-before -p 'Kill Claude pane and run recall? (y/n)' 'respawn-pane -k \"/opt/homebrew/bin/recall\"'" \
     "source-file ~/.tmux.conf \\; display '#[fg=#a6e3a1]󰑓 Config Reloaded#[default]'"

Index: scripts/tmux/tokyo-night-custom.sh
===================================================================
--- scripts/tmux/tokyo-night-custom.sh	
+++ scripts/tmux/tokyo-night-custom.sh	
@@ -1,60 +1,60 @@
 #!/usr/bin/env bash
 # shellcheck disable=SC2034
 # =============================================================================
 # Theme: Tokyo Night Custom
-# Variant: Storm-based with personalised session/window colors
+# Variant: Storm-based with straight-edged, high-contrast status colors
 # Base: https://github.com/folke/tokyonight.nvim (storm palette)
 #
 # Edit these colors to customise your tmux status bar appearance.
 # After editing, reload tmux config: prefix + r (or tmux source ~/.tmux.conf)
 #
 # Color reference (Tokyo Night palette):
 #   Blue:    #7aa2f7   Purple: #bb9af7   Cyan:   #7dcfff
 #   Green:   #9ece6a   Yellow: #e0af68   Orange: #ff9e64
 #   Red:     #f7768e   Teal:   #73daca   Pink:   #ff007c
 #   Fg:      #c0caf5   Dim:    #565f89   Bg:     #24283b
 # =============================================================================
 
 declare -gA THEME_COLORS
 
-THEME_COLORS[statusbar-bg]="#292e42"
-THEME_COLORS[statusbar-fg]="#c0caf5"
+THEME_COLORS["statusbar-bg"]="#1f2335"
+THEME_COLORS["statusbar-fg"]="#c0caf5"
 
-# Session (left pill)
-THEME_COLORS[session-bg]="#9ece6a"
-THEME_COLORS[session-fg]="#1a1b26"
-THEME_COLORS[session-prefix-bg]="#e0af68"
-THEME_COLORS[session-copy-bg]="#7dcfff"
+# Session (left segment)
+THEME_COLORS["session-bg"]="#bb9af7"
+THEME_COLORS["session-fg"]="#1a1b26"
+THEME_COLORS["session-prefix-bg"]="#ff9e64"
+THEME_COLORS["session-copy-bg"]="#73daca"
 
-# Windows (centre pills)
-# Active window: #bb9af7 (purple) is storm default
+# Windows (centre segments)
+# Active window: #73daca (teal) keeps focus distinct from the session segment.
 # Try: #7aa2f7 (blue), #73daca (teal), #9ece6a (green), #ff9e64 (orange)
-THEME_COLORS[window-active-base]="#7aa2f7"
+THEME_COLORS["window-active-base"]="#73daca"
 # Inactive window: #3b4261 is storm default
-THEME_COLORS[window-inactive-base]="#3b4261"
+THEME_COLORS["window-inactive-base"]="#2f3549"
 
 # Pane borders
-THEME_COLORS[pane-border-active]="#7aa2f7"
-THEME_COLORS[pane-border-inactive]="#3b4261"
+THEME_COLORS["pane-border-active"]="#bb9af7"
+THEME_COLORS["pane-border-inactive"]="#2f3549"
 
 # Health states (variants auto-generated)
-THEME_COLORS[ok-base]="#394b70"
-THEME_COLORS[good-base]="#9ece6a"
-THEME_COLORS[info-base]="#7dcfff"
-THEME_COLORS[warning-base]="#e0af68"
-THEME_COLORS[error-base]="#f7768e"
-THEME_COLORS[disabled-base]="#565f89"
+THEME_COLORS["ok-base"]="#3d59a1"
+THEME_COLORS["good-base"]="#9ece6a"
+THEME_COLORS["info-base"]="#7dcfff"
+THEME_COLORS["warning-base"]="#ff9e64"
+THEME_COLORS["error-base"]="#f7768e"
+THEME_COLORS["disabled-base"]="#565f89"
 
 # Messages
-THEME_COLORS[message-bg]="#292e42"
-THEME_COLORS[message-fg]="#c0caf5"
+THEME_COLORS["message-bg"]="#1f2335"
+THEME_COLORS["message-fg"]="#c0caf5"
 
 # Popup and menu
-THEME_COLORS[popup-bg]="#292e42"
-THEME_COLORS[popup-fg]="#c0caf5"
-THEME_COLORS[popup-border]="#7aa2f7"
-THEME_COLORS[menu-bg]="#292e42"
-THEME_COLORS[menu-fg]="#c0caf5"
-THEME_COLORS[menu-selected-bg]="#9ece6a"
-THEME_COLORS[menu-selected-fg]="#24283b"
-THEME_COLORS[menu-border]="#7aa2f7"
+THEME_COLORS["popup-bg"]="#1f2335"
+THEME_COLORS["popup-fg"]="#c0caf5"
+THEME_COLORS["popup-border"]="#bb9af7"
+THEME_COLORS["menu-bg"]="#1f2335"
+THEME_COLORS["menu-fg"]="#c0caf5"
+THEME_COLORS["menu-selected-bg"]="#73daca"
+THEME_COLORS["menu-selected-fg"]="#24283b"
+THEME_COLORS["menu-border"]="#bb9af7"

parts.text: Can we modify out tmux status bar so the colours are different and the edges aren't curved?

**Editing tmux**

I need to inspect and edit tmux. It feels like I should dive into the settings and configurations to see what needs changing. Maybe I could look at the key bindings or session settings? I want to make sure I thoroughly understand the context before making any adjustments. So, I’ll take a moment to gather what I might need to change and ensure I’m clear on the desired outcome.
I’ll inspect the existing tmux status configuration first so the change stays consistent with your current theme and plugin setup.

36: # Combined with the session-closed hook below, this guarantees a fallback

121: if-shell "uname | grep -q Darwin" \

302: set -g @powerkit_datetime_format "%H:%M %a %d %b"  # Time and date format
303: set -g @powerkit_git_show_branch true         # Show current Git branch
304: set -g @powerkit_git_show_status true         # Show Git status indicators (*, +, etc.)
305: set -g @powerkit_bandwidth_interface "auto"   # Auto-detect network interface
306: set -g @powerkit_wifi_show_signal true        # Show WiFi signal strength
307: 
308: # Catppuccin Mocha Theme with omerxx-inspired aesthetics
309: 
310: # Session Management - Single unified manager with tmuxinator integration
311: # Access with: Ctrl-s + S
312: bind S display-popup -E -h 80% -w 80% "$HOME/dotfiles/scripts/tmux/tmux-session-manager.sh"
313: 
314: # Session Trash Recovery - Browse and restore killed sessions
315: # Access with: Ctrl-s + T
316: bind T display-popup -E -h 80% -w 80% "$HOME/dotfiles/scripts/tmux/tmux-session-trash.sh restore"
317: 
318: # Which-key configuration (disabled - conflicts with Space binding)
319: # set -g @which-key-popup 1
320: 
321: # =============================================================================
322: # STATUS BAR CONFIGURATION - Three Section Layout
323: # =============================================================================
324: # ① Session (left) | ② Plugins (center) | ③ Windows (right/center)
325: # Powered by tmux-powerkit with rounded, pill-shaped segments
326: # =============================================================================
327: 
328: # Status bar layout configuration
329: set -g status-justify centre              # Center window list
330: set -g status-left-length 50              # Space for session indicator
331: set -g status-right-length 150            # Space for plugin pills
332: 
333: # Background styling (powerkit themes will override with their colors)
334: set -g status-style "bg=#1e1e2e,fg=#cdd6f4"
335: 
336: # Ensure pane backgrounds are not forced to a solid color
337: # (lets terminal/wezterm transparency show through and enables nvim winblend)
338: set -g window-style 'bg=default'
339: set -g window-active-style 'bg=default'
340: set -g popup-style 'bg=default,fg=default'
341: 
342: # Window status styling (powerkit may override, but provides fallback)
343: # Active window - highlighted with rounded background
344: setw -g window-status-current-style "fg=#1e1e2e,bg=#89b4fa,bold"
345: # Inactive windows - subdued appearance
346: setw -g window-status-style "fg=#6c7086,bg=#313244"
347: 
348: # Pane borders - subtle with accent on active
349: set -g pane-border-style "fg=#313244"
350: set -g pane-active-border-style "fg=#cba6f7"
351: 
352: # Message style
353: set -g message-style "bg=#313244,fg=#cdd6f4"
354: set -g message-command-style "bg=#313244,fg=#cdd6f4"
355: 
356: # Copy mode colors
357: set -g mode-style "bg=#45475a,fg=#cdd6f4"
358: 
359: # # Update status bar every 30 seconds for CPU/RAM (OPTIMIZED: reduced from 10s)
360: # # This reduces refresh overhead by 3x while still providing timely updates
361: # set -g status-interval 30
362: 
363: # Auto-install TPM if not present
364: if "test ! -d ~/.tmux/plugins/tpm" \
365:    "run 'git clone https://github.com/tmux-plugins/tpm ~/.tmux/plugins/tpm && ~/.tmux/plugins/tpm/bin/install_plugins'"
366: 
367: # Automatically split new windows and run claude (DISABLED)
368: # This creates a vertical split with claude in the right pane for every new window
369: # set-hook -g after-new-window 'split-window -h -p 40 "claude"'
370: 
371: # Manual keybinding to open Claude Code when needed
372: # Prefix + C opens Claude in a vertical split (40% width on the right)
373: bind C split-window -h -p 40 "claude --dangerously-skip-permissions"
374: 
375: # Quick launch: Ctrl-s a opens Claude with smart adaptive split
376: bind a if-shell -F "#{e|<:#{e|*:8,#{pane_width}},#{e|*:20,#{pane_height}}}" \
377:   "splitw -v -c '#{pane_current_path}' 'claude --dangerously-skip-permissions --effort max --remote-control' ; display '#[fg=#a6e3a1]↔ Split Horizontal (Claude)#[default]'" \
378:   "splitw -h -c '#{pane_current_path}' 'claude --dangerously-skip-permissions --effort max --remote-control' ; display '#[fg=#89dceb]↕ Split Vertical (Claude)#[default]'"
379: 
380: # Quick launch: Ctrl-s O opens OpenCode in its allow-permissions setup
381: bind O if-shell -F "#{e|<:#{e|*:8,#{pane_width}},#{e|*:20,#{pane_height}}}" \
382:   "splitw -v -c '#{pane_current_path}' '$HOME/dotfiles/scripts/opencode/tmux-open.sh' ; display '#[fg=#a6e3a1]↔ Split Horizontal (OpenCode)#[default]'" \
383:   "splitw -h -c '#{pane_current_path}' '$HOME/dotfiles/scripts/opencode/tmux-open.sh' ; display '#[fg=#89dceb]↕ Split Vertical (OpenCode)#[default]'"
384: 
385: # Quick launch: Ctrl-s e opens Neovim in current directory
386: # C-c first to normalize Fish vi-mode state (lands us in insert mode at empty
387: # prompt) so the typed `nvim .` isn't eaten as vi-mode normal commands.
388: bind e send-keys C-c "nvim ." Enter
389: 
390: # Quick launch: Ctrl-s E opens per-directory gwtt-prompt.local.md with smart adaptive split
391: bind E if-shell -F "#{e|<:#{e|*:8,#{pane_width}},#{e|*:20,#{pane_height}}}" \
392:   "splitw -v -c '#{pane_current_path}' '$HOME/dotfiles/scripts/tmux/gwtt-prompt-edit.sh' ; display '#[fg=#a6e3a1]↔ Split Horizontal (gwtt-prompt)#[default]'" \
393:   "splitw -h -c '#{pane_current_path}' '$HOME/dotfiles/scripts/tmux/gwtt-prompt-edit.sh' ; display '#[fg=#89dceb]↕ Split Vertical (gwtt-prompt)#[default]'"
394: 
395: # URL handler binding (smart: tries visible pane first, falls back to full scrollback)
396: bind-key o run-shell -b "$HOME/dotfiles/scripts/tmux/tmux-url-handler.sh"
397: 
398: # Keep default tmux bindings for navigation
399: # Prefix + O: select previous pane
400: # Prefix + p: previous window (default tmux behavior)
401: 
402: # Initialize TMUX plugin manager (keep this line at the very bottom of tmux.conf)
403: run '~/.tmux/plugins/tpm/tpm'
404: 
405: # Force tmux-open bindings after all plugins load
406: run-shell '~/.tmux/plugins/tmux-open/open.tmux'
407: 
408: # Restore prefix+x to kill-pane (extrakto plugin overrides this even with @extrakto_key changed)
409: # Enhanced: capture window info before killing for worktree auto-cleanup.
410: # Run the wrapper in tmux background so pane close stays responsive.
411: bind-key x run-shell -b '~/dotfiles/scripts/tmux/tmux-kill-pane-cleanup.sh "#{session_name}" "#{window_name}" "#{window_panes}"'
412: 
413: # Kill window: prefix + X (Shift-x) - closes entire window with worktree cleanup
414: bind-key X confirm-before -p 'Kill window "#{window_name}"? (y/n)' \
415:   "run-shell -b '~/dotfiles/scripts/tmux/tmux-worktree-cleanup.sh \"#{session_name}\" \"#{window_name}\"'; kill-window"
416: 
417: # Force status bar position and layout (override plugin defaults)
418: set -g status-position bottom
419: set -g status-justify centre
420: 
421: # Final overrides to keep transparent panes/popups after plugins load
422: set -g window-style 'bg=default'
423: set -g window-active-style 'bg=default'
424: set -g popup-style 'bg=default,fg=default'
425: 
426: # Override powerkit's default 5s status-interval (must be after TPM)
427: set -g status-interval 15
428: 
429: # Fix nested fish: reset default-command to empty after TPM loads.
430: # tmux-sensible (loaded by TPM) overrides empty default-command with
431: # "reattach-to-user-namespace -l $SHELL". By setting it to fish BEFORE TPM,
432: # sensible sees non-empty value and leaves it alone. Now we reset to empty
433: # so tmux launches default-shell directly (no "fish -c fish" nesting).
434: set -g default-command ''
435: 
436: # Reassert clipboard copy-mode bindings after TPM plugins load.
437: # tmux-yank can override these with legacy reattach-to-user-namespace commands.
438: if-shell "uname | grep -q Darwin" \
439:   "bind-key -T copy-mode-vi y send-keys -X copy-pipe-and-cancel 'pbcopy'; \
440:    bind-key -T copy-mode-vi MouseDragEnd1Pane send-keys -X copy-pipe-and-cancel 'pbcopy'; \
441:    bind-key -T copy-mode-vi Enter send-keys -X copy-pipe-and-cancel 'pbcopy'" \
442:   "bind-key -T copy-mode-vi y send-keys -X copy-pipe-and-cancel 'xclip -selection clipboard'; \
443:    bind-key -T copy-mode-vi MouseDragEnd1Pane send-keys -X copy-pipe-and-cancel 'xclip -selection clipboard'; \
444:    bind-key -T copy-mode-vi Enter send-keys -X copy-pipe-and-cancel 'xclip -selection clipboard'"
445: 
446: # Inject @wname_style into status bar window tabs (must be after TPM/powerkit)
447: # Makes agent state colors visible in the status bar, not just choose-tree
448: run-shell '~/dotfiles/scripts/tmux/inject-wname-style.sh'
449: 
450: # Colored choose-tree session/window picker (must be after TPM)
451: # Tree labels (session/window names, markers) use pane fg — set via select-pane -P
452: # pane-mode-changed hook resets pane style when exiting choose-tree
453: # Agent colors set by watcher via @wname_style: running=green, idle=yellow, stuck=red
454: set-hook -g pane-mode-changed[100] 'if -F "#{pane_in_mode}" "" "select-pane -P default"'
455: bind s select-pane -P 'fg=#9ece6a' \; choose-tree -Zw -F '#{?pane_format,#[fg=#7dcfff]#{pane_current_command} #[fg=#565f89](#{pane_current_path}),#{?window_format,#{@wname_style}#{window_name}#{?window_active, *,},#[fg=#565f89]#{session_windows} windows#{?session_attached, #[fg=#e0af68](attached),}}}'
456: bind w select-pane -P 'fg=#9ece6a' \; choose-tree -Zw -F '#{?pane_format,#[fg=#7dcfff]#{pane_current_command} #[fg=#565f89](#{pane_current_path}),#{?window_format,#{@wname_style}#{window_name}#{?window_active, *,},#[fg=#565f89]#{session_windows} windows#{?session_attached, #[fg=#e0af68](attached),}}}'
457: 
458: # Context-aware r key: recall when in Claude, config reload otherwise
459: # Must be after TPM to override tmux-powerkit's reload binding
460: bind r if-shell "ps -o args= -t #{pane_tty} 2>/dev/null | grep -q 'claude'" \
461:     "confirm-before -p 'Kill Claude pane and run recall? (y/n)' 'respawn-pane -k \"/opt/homebrew/bin/recall\"'" \
462:     "source-file ~/.tmux.conf \\; display '#[fg=#a6e3a1]󰑓 Config Reloaded#[default]'"

(End of file - total 462 lines)
</content>

parts.text:
**Inspecting theme script**

I see I need to take a closer look at the theme script. It’s crucial to check everything for any issues or areas for improvement. It’s a good idea to examine the overall functionality, layout, and any specific elements that might not be working as intended. I wonder if there are any specific features that need attention, and I’ll be thorough to ensure everything is in tip-top shape! Let's get started on this!

The curved edges come from `@powerkit_separator_style "rounded"` and the colors are driven by `scripts/tmux/tokyo-night-custom.sh`. I’m going to switch separators to a straight style and adjust the custom palette rather than replacing the status framework.

Instructions from: /Users/shahe.islam/dotfiles/scripts/CLAUDE.md
# Scripts Index

112 scripts and 20 subdirectories supporting the dotfiles ecosystem. Scripts use `#!/usr/bin/env bash` with `set -euo pipefail`.

## Key Entry Points

| Script | Purpose |
|--------|---------|
| `setup.sh` | Main machine bootstrap (Homebrew, stow, tools, configs) |
| `setup-mobile-coding.sh` | Mosh + Tailscale for remote access |
| `setup-selfhost-llm.sh` | Ollama + Open WebUI local LLM |

## Subdirectories

### Core Infrastructure
| Directory | Purpose |
|-----------|---------|
| `setup/` | macOS defaults, system preferences |
| `harness/` | Drift detection, diagnostics for dotfiles health |
| `lib/` | Shared bash libraries and utilities |
| `bin/` | Standalone executables added to PATH |
| `hooks/` | Git hooks and CI integration |
| `ci-hooks/` | CI-specific hook scripts |
| `tests/` | Shell script tests |
| `profiles/` | Shell profile management |

### Agent & AI
| Directory | Purpose |
|-----------|---------|
| `claude/` | Claude Code helper scripts |
| `codex/` | Codex CLI integration and account management |
| `otel/` | OpenTelemetry LGTM stack (docker-compose, Grafana dashboards) |
| `obsidian/` | Obsidian vault management scripts |
| `openclaw/` | OpenClaw AI platform config |

### DevOps & Containers
| Directory | Purpose |
|-----------|---------|
| `docker/` | Dockerfile and container testing |
| `devcontainer/` | Devcontainer credential export, auto-login |
| `kubernetes/` | K8s helper scripts |
| `manifests/` | K8s manifest files (with README) |
| `linux/` | Linux-specific setup scripts |
| `windows/` | Windows/WSL setup |
| `windows-vm/` | Windows VM provisioning |

### Domain-Specific
| Directory | Purpose |
|-----------|---------|
| `youtube/` | YouTube transcript fetcher (yt-transcript.py) |
| `anki/` | Anki flashcard generation |
| `cv/` | CV compilation and LaTeX tooling |
| `aws/` | AWS helper scripts |
| `tmux/` | tmux watcher, session management |
| `pihole/` | Pi-hole DNS management |
| `ticket-queue/` | Ticket queue management |
| `tools/` | Standalone tool scripts |

## Conventions

- Bash scripts: `set -euo pipefail`, quote all variables, snake_case functions
- File naming: kebab-case (e.g., `setup-mobile-coding.sh`)
- Shared code: import from `lib/` directory
- Never use `((var++))` with `set -e` — use `var=$((var + 1))` instead

</system-reminder>

1: #!/usr/bin/env bash
2: # =============================================================================
3: # PowerKit Renderer: Separator
4: # Description: Powerline separator glyph management and transition logic
5: # =============================================================================
6: # This module handles:
7: # - Separator glyphs (powerline characters)
8: # - Transition colors between elements (window-to-window, session-to-window)
9: # - Spacing segments for visual gaps
10: # - Direction-aware separator rendering (left vs right side of status bar)
11: # =============================================================================
12: 
13: # Source guard
14: POWERKIT_ROOT="${POWERKIT_ROOT:-$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")/../.." && pwd)}"
15: . "${POWERKIT_ROOT}/src/core/guard.sh"
16: source_guard "renderer_separator" && return 0
17: 
18: . "${POWERKIT_ROOT}/src/core/defaults.sh"
19: . "${POWERKIT_ROOT}/src/core/options.sh"
20: 
21: # =============================================================================
22: # Separator Style Configuration
23: # =============================================================================
24: # Powerline glyphs are defined in defaults.sh as POWERKIT_SEP_* constants
25: 
26: # Per-cycle cache for separator characters (avoids repeated tmux option lookups)
27: declare -g _SEP_CACHE_STYLE=""
28: declare -g _SEP_CACHE_EDGE_STYLE=""
29: declare -g _SEP_CACHE_EDGE_APPLY_ALL=""
30: declare -g _SEP_CACHE_INITIAL_STYLE=""
31: declare -g _SEP_CACHE_LEFT=""
32: declare -g _SEP_CACHE_RIGHT=""
33: declare -g _SEP_CACHE_INITIAL=""
34: declare -g _SEP_CACHE_FINAL=""
35: declare -g _SEP_CACHE_SPACING_MODE=""
36: declare -g _SEP_CACHE_EDGE_LEFT=""
37: declare -g _SEP_CACHE_EDGE_RIGHT=""
38: 
39: # Reset separator cache (call at start of each render cycle along with cache_reset_cycle)
40: separator_reset_cache() {
41:     _SEP_CACHE_STYLE=""
42:     _SEP_CACHE_EDGE_STYLE=""
43:     _SEP_CACHE_EDGE_APPLY_ALL=""
44:     _SEP_CACHE_INITIAL_STYLE=""
45:     _SEP_CACHE_LEFT=""
46:     _SEP_CACHE_RIGHT=""
47:     _SEP_CACHE_INITIAL=""
48:     _SEP_CACHE_FINAL=""
49:     _SEP_CACHE_SPACING_MODE=""
50:     _SEP_CACHE_EDGE_LEFT=""
51:     _SEP_CACHE_EDGE_RIGHT=""
52: }
53: 
54: # Pre-populate separator cache (call early in render cycle to avoid subshell losses)
55: # After calling this, use $_SEP_CACHE_LEFT, $_SEP_CACHE_RIGHT directly instead of
56: # $(get_left_separator), $(get_right_separator) to avoid subshell overhead.
57: # Usage: separator_ensure_cache
58: separator_ensure_cache() {
59:     # Ensure style is loaded first
60:     [[ -z "$_SEP_CACHE_STYLE" ]] && _SEP_CACHE_STYLE=$(get_tmux_option "@powerkit_separator_style" "${POWERKIT_DEFAULT_SEPARATOR_STYLE}")
61: 
62:     # Pre-populate all separator glyphs
63:     [[ -z "$_SEP_CACHE_LEFT" ]] && _SEP_CACHE_LEFT=$(_get_separator_glyph "$_SEP_CACHE_STYLE" "left")
64:     [[ -z "$_SEP_CACHE_RIGHT" ]] && _SEP_CACHE_RIGHT=$(_get_separator_glyph "$_SEP_CACHE_STYLE" "right")
65: 
66:     # Edge style - parse :all suffix for apply-all-edges feature
67:     [[ -z "$_SEP_CACHE_EDGE_STYLE" ]] && {
68:         local raw_style
69:         raw_style=$(get_tmux_option "@powerkit_edge_separator_style" "${POWERKIT_DEFAULT_EDGE_SEPARATOR_STYLE}")
70:         # Remove :all suffix if present and cache the flag
71:         if [[ "$raw_style" == *":all" ]]; then
72:             _SEP_CACHE_EDGE_APPLY_ALL="true"
73:             _SEP_CACHE_EDGE_STYLE="${raw_style%%:all}"
74:         else
75:             _SEP_CACHE_EDGE_APPLY_ALL="false"
76:             _SEP_CACHE_EDGE_STYLE="$raw_style"
77:         fi
78:         [[ "$_SEP_CACHE_EDGE_STYLE" == "same" ]] && _SEP_CACHE_EDGE_STYLE="$_SEP_CACHE_STYLE"
79:     }
80: 
81:     [[ -z "$_SEP_CACHE_FINAL" ]] && _SEP_CACHE_FINAL=$(_get_separator_glyph "$_SEP_CACHE_EDGE_STYLE" "right")
82: 
83:     # Initial style
84:     [[ -z "$_SEP_CACHE_INITIAL_STYLE" ]] && {
85:         local style
86:         style=$(get_tmux_option "@powerkit_initial_separator_style" "")
87:         [[ -z "$style" || "$style" == "same" ]] && style="$_SEP_CACHE_EDGE_STYLE"
88:         _SEP_CACHE_INITIAL_STYLE="$style"
89:     }
90: 
91:     [[ -z "$_SEP_CACHE_INITIAL" ]] && _SEP_CACHE_INITIAL=$(_get_separator_glyph "$_SEP_CACHE_INITIAL_STYLE" "left")
92: 
93:     # Spacing mode
94:     [[ -z "$_SEP_CACHE_SPACING_MODE" ]] && _SEP_CACHE_SPACING_MODE=$(get_tmux_option "@powerkit_elements_spacing" "${POWERKIT_DEFAULT_ELEMENTS_SPACING}")
95: 
96:     # Edge separator glyphs
97:     [[ -z "$_SEP_CACHE_EDGE_LEFT" ]] && _SEP_CACHE_EDGE_LEFT=$(_get_separator_glyph "$_SEP_CACHE_EDGE_STYLE" "left")
98:     [[ -z "$_SEP_CACHE_EDGE_RIGHT" ]] && _SEP_CACHE_EDGE_RIGHT=$(_get_separator_glyph "$_SEP_CACHE_EDGE_STYLE" "right")
99: }
100: 
101: # Get separator style from options (cached per-cycle)
102: get_separator_style() {
103:     if [[ -z "$_SEP_CACHE_STYLE" ]]; then
104:         _SEP_CACHE_STYLE=$(get_tmux_option "@powerkit_separator_style" "${POWERKIT_DEFAULT_SEPARATOR_STYLE}")
105:     fi
106:     printf '%s' "$_SEP_CACHE_STYLE"
107: }
108: 
109: # Get edge separator style (boundaries: end of windows, start of plugins)
110: # Returns the style to use for edge separators (without :all suffix), or main style if "same"
111: # Parses :all suffix and caches the apply-all flag
112: # Usage: get_edge_separator_style
113: get_edge_separator_style() {
114:     if [[ -z "$_SEP_CACHE_EDGE_STYLE" ]]; then
115:         local raw_style
116:         raw_style=$(get_tmux_option "@powerkit_edge_separator_style" "${POWERKIT_DEFAULT_EDGE_SEPARATOR_STYLE}")
117: 
118:         # Remove :all suffix if present and cache the flag
119:         if [[ "$raw_style" == *":all" ]]; then
120:             _SEP_CACHE_EDGE_APPLY_ALL="true"
121:             _SEP_CACHE_EDGE_STYLE="${raw_style%%:all}"
122:         else
123:             _SEP_CACHE_EDGE_APPLY_ALL="false"
124:             _SEP_CACHE_EDGE_STYLE="$raw_style"
125:         fi
126: 
127:         [[ "$_SEP_CACHE_EDGE_STYLE" == "same" ]] && _SEP_CACHE_EDGE_STYLE=$(get_separator_style)
128:     fi
129:     printf '%s' "$_SEP_CACHE_EDGE_STYLE"
130: }
131: 
132: # Get initial separator style (first plugin in status-right)
133: # Returns the style to use for first plugin separator, defaults to edge style for symmetry (cached per-cycle)
134: get_initial_separator_style() {
135:     if [[ -z "$_SEP_CACHE_INITIAL_STYLE" ]]; then
136:         local style
137:         style=$(get_tmux_option "@powerkit_initial_separator_style" "")
138: 
139:         # If not set, use edge_separator_style for visual symmetry
140:         if [[ -z "$style" || "$style" == "same" ]]; then
141:             _SEP_CACHE_INITIAL_STYLE=$(get_edge_separator_style)
142:         else
143:             _SEP_CACHE_INITIAL_STYLE="$style"
144:         fi
145:     fi
146:     printf '%s' "$_SEP_CACHE_INITIAL_STYLE"
147: }
148: 
149: # =============================================================================
150: # Separator Character Functions
151: # =============================================================================
152: 
153: # Get separator glyph for a specific style and direction
154: # Usage: _get_separator_glyph "style" "direction"
155: # direction: "left" or "right"
156: _get_separator_glyph() {
157:     local style="$1"
158:     local direction="${2:-right}"
159: 
160:     if [[ "$direction" == "left" ]]; then
161:         case "$style" in
162:             normal)           printf '%s' "$POWERKIT_SEP_SOLID_LEFT" ;;
163:             rounded)          printf '%s' "$POWERKIT_SEP_ROUND_LEFT" ;;
164:             slant)            printf '%s' "$POWERKIT_SEP_SLANT_LEFT" ;;
165:             slantup|slant-up) printf '%s' "$POWERKIT_SEP_SLANT_UP_LEFT" ;;
166:             flame)            printf '%s' "$POWERKIT_SEP_FLAME_LEFT" ;;
167:             pixel)            printf '%s' "$POWERKIT_SEP_PIXEL_LEFT" ;;
168:             honeycomb)        printf '%s' "$POWERKIT_SEP_HONEYCOMB_LEFT" ;;
169:             trapezoid)        printf '%s' "$POWERKIT_SEP_TRAPEZOID_LEFT" ;;
170:             none)             printf '' ;;
171:             *)                printf '%s' "$POWERKIT_SEP_ROUND_LEFT" ;;
172:         esac
173:     else
174:         case "$style" in
175:             normal)           printf '%s' "$POWERKIT_SEP_SOLID_RIGHT" ;;
176:             rounded)          printf '%s' "$POWERKIT_SEP_ROUND_RIGHT" ;;
177:             slant)            printf '%s' "$POWERKIT_SEP_SLANT_RIGHT" ;;
178:             slantup|slant-up) printf '%s' "$POWERKIT_SEP_SLANT_UP_RIGHT" ;;
179:             flame)            printf '%s' "$POWERKIT_SEP_FLAME_RIGHT" ;;
180:             pixel)            printf '%s' "$POWERKIT_SEP_PIXEL_RIGHT" ;;
181:             honeycomb)        printf '%s' "$POWERKIT_SEP_HONEYCOMB_RIGHT" ;;
182:             trapezoid)        printf '%s' "$POWERKIT_SEP_TRAPEZOID_RIGHT" ;;
183:             none)             printf '' ;;
184:             *)                printf '%s' "$POWERKIT_SEP_ROUND_RIGHT" ;;
185:         esac
186:     fi
187: }
188: 
189: # Get LEFT separator character (points left ◀, used in status-right) (cached per-cycle)
190: # Usage: get_left_separator
191: get_left_separator() {
192:     if [[ -z "$_SEP_CACHE_LEFT" ]]; then
193:         _SEP_CACHE_LEFT=$(_get_separator_glyph "$(get_separator_style)" "left")
194:     fi
195:     printf '%s' "$_SEP_CACHE_LEFT"
196: }
197: 
198: # Get RIGHT separator character (points right ▶, used in status-left and windows) (cached per-cycle)
199: # Usage: get_right_separator
200: get_right_separator() {

204:     printf '%s' "$_SEP_CACHE_RIGHT"
205: }
206: 
207: # Get FINAL separator character (end of window list, uses edge style) (cached per-cycle)

209: get_final_separator() {

1: #!/usr/bin/env bash
2: # =============================================================================
3: # PowerKit Core: Defaults Configuration

5: # Description: All default values for PowerKit configuration options
6: #
7: # This file defines all default values that users can override via tmux.conf.
8: # Each option is documented with its purpose, valid values, and examples.
9: #
10: # Override syntax in tmux.conf:

12: #   set -g @powerkit_plugin_<plugin>_<option> "value"

126: # Available plugins (44 total):
127: #   System Monitoring:
128: #     battery, cpu, disk, fan, gpu, iops, loadavg, memory, swap, temperature, uptime,
129: #     volume, brightness
130: #   Network:
131: #     external_ip, netspeed, ping, vpn, weather, wifi, ssh
132: #   Development:
133: #     chezmoi, git, github, gitlab, bitbucket, jira, kubernetes, terraform, cloud
134: #   Media:
135: #     nowplaying, audiodevices, camera, microphone
136: #   Productivity:
137: #     datetime, timezones, pomodoro, smartkey, bitwarden
138: #   Financial:
139: #     crypto, stocks
140: #   Services:
141: #     cloudstatus, packages, bluetooth, hostname
142: #
143: # Plugin Groups:
144: #   Use group(...) syntax to visually group plugins with a shared background:
145: #     group(cpu,memory)           - Groups plugins with automatic group color
146: #     group(git,github,gitlab)    - Another group with next color from palette
147: #
148: #   Example with groups:
149: #     set -g @powerkit_plugins "group(cpu,memory),group(git,github),datetime"
150: #
151: #   Groups are colored automatically using @powerkit_plugin_group_colors palette.
152: #   Each group gets the next color from the palette in order.
153: POWERKIT_DEFAULT_PLUGINS="datetime,battery,cpu,memory,hostname,git"
154: 
155: # @powerkit_plugin_group_colors - Color palette for plugin groups
156: # Comma-separated list of theme colors or hex values
157: # Groups are assigned colors in order from this palette
158: # Colors are used for separator backgrounds between plugins in the same group.
159: # When @powerkit_plugin_group_coloring is enabled, these colors also override
160: # plugin segment backgrounds (health is then indicated via bold text styling).
161: # Values: theme color names (with -darker variant) or hex colors
162: # Default palette uses theme colors for visual harmony:
163: #   info-base-darker          - Blue tones (network, info plugins)
164: #   window-active-base-darker - Purple tones (development plugins)
165: #   ok-base-darker            - Green tones (status, success plugins)
166: #   warning-base-darker       - Yellow tones (alert plugins)
167: #   error-base-darker         - Red tones (critical plugins)
168: #   disabled-base             - Gray tones (neutral plugins)
169: POWERKIT_DEFAULT_PLUGIN_GROUP_COLORS="info-base-darker,window-active-base-darker,ok-base-darker,warning-base-darker,error-base-darker,disabled-base"
170: 
171: # @powerkit_plugin_group_coloring - Apply group palette colors to plugin segments
172: # Values: "true", "false"
173: # When false (default): groups affect separator grouping only; plugins use health-based colors
174: # When true: group palette colors override health-based segment backgrounds;
175: #   health is indicated through bold text styling instead
176: POWERKIT_DEFAULT_PLUGIN_GROUP_COLORING="false"
177:

179: # STATUS BAR CONFIGURATION
180: # =============================================================================

#!/usr/bin/env bash
# =============================================================================
# PowerKit Core: Defaults Configuration
# =============================================================================
# Description: All default values for PowerKit configuration options
#
# This file defines all default values that users can override via tmux.conf.
# Each option is documented with its purpose, valid values, and examples.
#
# Override syntax in tmux.conf:
#   set -g @powerkit_<option> "value"
#   set -g @powerkit_plugin_<plugin>_<option> "value"
#
# shellcheck disable=SC2034
# =============================================================================

# Source guard
POWERKIT_ROOT="${POWERKIT_ROOT:-$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")/../.." && pwd)}"
. "${POWERKIT_ROOT}/src/core/guard.sh"
source_guard "defaults" && return 0

<content>
180: # =============================================================================
181: # @powerkit_status_interval - Refresh interval in seconds
182: # Values: any positive integer
183: # Lower values = more responsive but higher CPU usage
184: # Recommended: 5 (balanced), 2 (responsive), 10 (low CPU)
185: POWERKIT_DEFAULT_STATUS_INTERVAL="5"
186: 
187: # @powerkit_status_position - Status bar position
188: # Values: "top", "bottom"
189: POWERKIT_DEFAULT_STATUS_POSITION="top"
190: 
191: # @powerkit_status_justify - Window list alignment
192: # Values: "left", "centre", "right"
193: POWERKIT_DEFAULT_STATUS_JUSTIFY="left"
194: 
195: # Internal: Maximum length for status-left and status-right
196: POWERKIT_DEFAULT_STATUS_LEFT_LENGTH="100"
197: POWERKIT_DEFAULT_STATUS_RIGHT_LENGTH="500"
198: 
199: # @powerkit_bar_layout - Status bar layout mode
200: # Values:
201: #   "single" - Traditional single status line (default)
202: #   "double" - Two status lines: Line 0 = Session + Windows, Line 1 = Plugins
203: POWERKIT_DEFAULT_BAR_LAYOUT="single"
204: 
205: # @powerkit_status_order - Element order in status bar
206: # Comma-separated list of: session, windows, plugins
207: #
208: # 2-element orders (auto-expanded with windows):
209: #   "session,plugins" - Session+windows LEFT, plugins RIGHT (default)
210: #   "plugins,session" - Plugins LEFT, windows+session RIGHT
211: #
212: # 3-element orders (enables CENTERED layout):
213: #   "session,windows,plugins" - Session LEFT, windows CENTER, plugins RIGHT
214: #   "plugins,windows,session" - Plugins LEFT, windows CENTER, session RIGHT
215: #   "session,plugins,windows" - Session LEFT, plugins CENTER, windows RIGHT
216: #
217: # Any element in the middle position will be centered in the status bar
218: POWERKIT_DEFAULT_STATUS_ORDER="session,plugins"
219: 
220: # =============================================================================
221: # LAZY LOADING (Stale-While-Revalidate)
222: # =============================================================================
223: # PowerKit uses a stale-while-revalidate caching strategy for fast UI response.
224: # When data is "stale" (older than TTL but within the stale window), cached data
225: # is shown immediately with visual indication while fresh data is fetched.
226: #
227: # Cache State Flow:
228: #   FRESH (age <= TTL)           -> Return cache, stale=0 (normal colors)
229: #   STALE (TTL < age <= TTL*M)   -> Return cache + bg refresh, stale=1 (darker colors)
230: #   VERY OLD (age > TTL*M)       -> Synchronous refresh (blocking), stale=0
231: #   COLLECTION FAILED            -> Return previous cache, stale=1 (darker colors)
232: #
233: # Where M = stale_multiplier
234: 
235: # @powerkit_lazy_loading - Enable stale-while-revalidate pattern
236: # Values: "true", "false"
237: # When enabled, stale cache data is returned immediately while fresh data
238: # is fetched in background. This prevents UI blocking on slow operations.
239: POWERKIT_DEFAULT_LAZY_LOADING="true"
240: 
241: # @powerkit_stale_multiplier - Maximum staleness as multiple of TTL
242: # Values: any positive integer
243: # Example: TTL=300s, multiplier=3 -> data up to 900s old can be returned
244: # After TTL * multiplier, synchronous (blocking) refresh is forced
245: POWERKIT_DEFAULT_STALE_MULTIPLIER="3"
246: 
247: # @powerkit_stale_color_variant - Visual indicator for stale data
248: # Values: "-darker", "-darkest", "-lighter", "-lightest"
249: # Applied to plugin background colors when displaying cached/stale data
250: # This provides visual feedback that data shown may be outdated
251: POWERKIT_DEFAULT_STALE_COLOR_VARIANT="-darkest"
252: 
253: # =============================================================================
254: # SEPARATOR CONFIGURATION
255: # =============================================================================
256: # @powerkit_separator_style - Main separator style between segments
257: # Values:
258: #   "normal"    - Solid powerline arrows ()
259: #   "rounded"   - Rounded powerline ()
260: #   "slant"     - Slanted diagonal ()
261: #   "slantup"   - Upward slant ()
262: #   "trapezoid" - Trapezoid shape ()
263: #   "flame"     - Flame/fire style ()
264: #   "pixel"     - Pixelated blocks ()
265: #   "honeycomb" - Hexagonal pattern ()
266: #   "none"      - No separators
267: POWERKIT_DEFAULT_SEPARATOR_STYLE="normal"
268: 
269: # @powerkit_edge_separator_style - Style for external edge separators
270: # Values:
271: #   "same"         - Use main separator_style (no special treatment)
272: #   "rounded"      - Rounded at standard edge points only
273: #   "rounded:all"  - Rounded at ALL external edges (session start/end, first/last window)
274: #   "flame:all"    - Flame at ALL external edges
275: #   ...any style (normal, rounded, slant, flame, etc.) with optional :all suffix
276: # The :all suffix applies the edge style to ALL external boundaries.
277: POWERKIT_DEFAULT_EDGE_SEPARATOR_STYLE="rounded"
278: 
279: # Powerline glyphs (using \U format for codes > 0xFF)
280: POWERKIT_SEP_SOLID_RIGHT=$'\U0000e0b0'
281: POWERKIT_SEP_SOLID_LEFT=$'\U0000e0b2'
282: POWERKIT_SEP_ROUND_RIGHT=$'\U0000e0b4'
283: POWERKIT_SEP_ROUND_LEFT=$'\U0000e0b6'
284: POWERKIT_SEP_SLANT_RIGHT=$'\U0000e0b8'
285: POWERKIT_SEP_SLANT_LEFT=$'\U0000e0ba'
286: POWERKIT_SEP_SLANT_UP_RIGHT=$'\U0000e0bc'
287: POWERKIT_SEP_SLANT_UP_LEFT=$'\U0000e0be'
288: POWERKIT_SEP_TRAPEZOID_RIGHT=$'\U0000e0c8'
289: POWERKIT_SEP_TRAPEZOID_LEFT=$'\U0000e0ca'
290: POWERKIT_SEP_FLAME_RIGHT=$'\U0000e0c0'
291: POWERKIT_SEP_FLAME_LEFT=$'\U0000e0c2'
292: POWERKIT_SEP_PIXEL_RIGHT=$'\U0000e0c4'
293: POWERKIT_SEP_PIXEL_LEFT=$'\U0000e0c6'
294: POWERKIT_SEP_HONEYCOMB_RIGHT=$'\U0000e0cc'
295: POWERKIT_SEP_HONEYCOMB_LEFT=$'\U0000e0cd'
296: 
297: # List of available separator styles (for validation)
298: POWERKIT_SEPARATOR_STYLES="normal rounded slant slantup trapezoid flame pixel honeycomb none"
299: 
300: # @powerkit_elements_spacing - Add gaps between status bar elements
301: # Values:
302: #   "false"   - No spacing (connected segments) - default
303: #   "true"    - Spacing everywhere
304: #   "both"    - Same as true
305: #   "windows" - Spacing only between windows
306: #   "plugins" - Spacing only between plugins
307: POWERKIT_DEFAULT_ELEMENTS_SPACING="false"
308: 
309: # @powerkit_icon_padding - Padding around plugin icons
310: # Controls spacing on each side of the icon within its segment.
311: # Helps normalize visual spacing for icons with different widths.
312: # Values:
313: #   1 - Minimal padding (1 space each side) - compact look
314: #   2 - Standard padding (2 spaces each side) - default, reduces width variation
315: #   3 - Wide padding (3 spaces each side) - very generous spacing
316: # Tip: If icons look inconsistently spaced, try increasing this value.
317: POWERKIT_DEFAULT_ICON_PADDING="1"
318: 
319: # Figure space character (U+2007) - same width as digits in most fonts
320: # Used for more consistent icon padding when standard spaces vary
321: POWERKIT_FIGURE_SPACE=$'\U00002007'
322: 
323: # =============================================================================
324: # SESSION CONFIGURATION
325: # =============================================================================
326: # @powerkit_session_icon - Session segment icon
327: # Values:
328: #   "auto" - Auto-detect OS icon (Apple for macOS, Tux for Linux, etc.)
329: #   Any Nerd Font icon character
330: POWERKIT_DEFAULT_SESSION_ICON="auto"
331: 
332: # @powerkit_session_prefix_icon - Icon when prefix key is pressed
333: POWERKIT_DEFAULT_SESSION_PREFIX_ICON=$'\U0000f11c'    # nf-fa-keyboard
334: 
335: # @powerkit_session_copy_icon - Icon in copy mode
336: POWERKIT_DEFAULT_SESSION_COPY_ICON=$'\U0000f0c5'      # nf-fa-copy
337:

**Considering shell commands**

I want to validate if shfmt auto-formatted my code because the system mentioned that it did. Should I run shellcheck? I might want to test tmux source too. There's a reading diff perspective to consider. Using git diff and bash -n could help, as well as potentially using the tmux source-file command. I need to be careful not to use separators. Maybe I can run a single command for an independent parallel status diff instead?

The config now has straight separators set explicitly for main, edge, and initial status transitions so PowerKit won’t fall back to rounded outer edges. I’m checking syntax and reloading tmux to verify it accepts the changes.

parts.text:
**Fixing bash array issues**

I noticed an autoformatting issue where my array keys changed to arithmetic. It seems like using `bash -n` didn't catch this. When declaring an associative array, having spaces in the keys is invalid. Looks like the assignment parser is involved here. The issue with `THEME_COLORS[statusbar - bg]` could be an arithmetic subscript error. I think the autoformatter messed things up, so I need to patch and quote the keys properly.
The syntax check passed parsing, but the diff exposed a formatter issue in the Bash associative-array keys. I’m correcting those keys to quoted form so the theme loads correctly at runtime.
