parts.text:
Updated tmux status bar.

Changed:
- `.tmux.conf`: `tmux-powerkit` separators from `rounded` to `normal`
- `.tmux.conf`: explicitly set edge and initial separators to `normal`
- `scripts/tmux/tokyo-night-custom.sh`: refreshed colors:
  - Purple session segment
  - Teal active window
  - Darker inactive/status background
  - Purple borders
  - Orange prefix/warning accents

Verified:
- `bash -n scripts/tmux/tokyo-night-custom.sh`
- Runtime theme source check
- `tmux source-file "$HOME/.tmux.conf"` succeeded

diffs.patch:
Index: .tmux.conf
===================================================================
--- .tmux.conf	
+++ .tmux.conf	
@@ -1,464 +1,464 @@
 # Basic tmux configuration
 set -g default-terminal "tmux-256color"
 set -ga terminal-overrides ",*:Tc:RGB,xterm-256color:Ms=\\E]52;c;%p2%s\\7,tmux-256color:Ms=\\E]52;c;%p2%s\\7"
 
 # Set prefix to Ctrl-s (more ergonomic than Ctrl-Space)
 set -g prefix C-s
 unbind C-b
 unbind C-a
 # Unbind C-z to ensure it's passed through to nested applications (like Neovim terminal)
 unbind C-z
 # Double tap Ctrl-s to send literal Ctrl-s to applications
 bind-key C-s send-prefix
 
 # Enable key repeat
 set -g repeat-time 0
 
 # Basic settings
 set -g mouse on
 set -g escape-time 0
 set -g focus-events on
 set -g history-limit 100000
 set -g base-index 1
 setw -g pane-base-index 1
 set -g renumber-windows on
 setw -g automatic-rename off
 
 # Prevent Claude Code nested-session false positives in tmux
 set-environment -gr CLAUDECODE
 
 # When a session is destroyed, switch to the most recently active session
 # instead of detaching the client (which would close the terminal).
 # Tested with tmux 3.5a.
 set -g detach-on-destroy off
 
 # Proactively ensure "main" session exists at server start.
 # Combined with the session-closed hook below, this guarantees a fallback
 # target is always available for detach-on-destroy.
 # exit-empty is set to off so the server survives the brief window between
 # sole-session destruction and hook recreation.
 set -g exit-empty off
 run-shell 'tmux has-session -t main 2>/dev/null || tmux new-session -d -s main'
 
 # Reactively recreate "main" if destroyed while the server is still running.
 set-hook -g session-closed 'if-shell "! tmux has-session -t main 2>/dev/null" "new-session -d -s main"'
 
 # Activity monitoring - custom completion detection
 # Monitors activity, then checks for completion after silence period
 setw -g monitor-activity on
 set -g visual-activity off  # Don't show visual alerts
 set -g activity-action any  # Trigger on ALL windows including current (enables Claude highlighting)
 # Disable visual styling for windows with activity (no grey background)
 set -g window-status-activity-style "fg=#6c7086,bg=#1e1e2e"
 
 # Disable silence monitoring (alert-silence hook doesn't fire reliably)
 setw -g monitor-silence 0
 set -g visual-silence off
 
 # Agent activity monitoring:
 #   Claude: event-driven via scripts/bin/claude wrapper (injects --settings hooks)
 #   Codex/Opencode: polling fallback via tmux-claude-watcher.sh daemon (10s interval)
 # Explicitly unset stale hooks from earlier config versions
 set-hook -gu alert-activity
 set-hook -gu after-select-window
 
 # Start watcher daemon for codex/opencode (claude is handled by the wrapper)
 run-shell '~/dotfiles/scripts/tmux/tmux-claude-watcher.sh start >/dev/null 2>&1 || true'
 
 # Bell on activity completion - disabled
 set -g bell-action none
 set -g visual-bell off  # Disable visual bell flash
 
 # Clipboard integration
 set -g set-clipboard on
 # Allow OSC-52 escape sequences to pass through to the terminal
 # Required for Neovim clipboard to work in SSH/container sessions
 set -g allow-passthrough on
 
 # OS-specific default shell configuration
 # reattach-to-user-namespace removed: not needed since macOS 12 (Monterey)
 # pbcopy/pbpaste and OSC-52 work natively in tmux on modern macOS
 # NOTE: default-command is set here to block tmux-sensible from overriding it
 # with "reattach-to-user-namespace -l $SHELL" (which spawns zsh, not fish).
 # After TPM loads, default-command is reset to empty (see post-TPM section)
 # so tmux launches default-shell directly without nesting.
 if-shell "uname | grep -q Darwin" \
   "set -g default-shell /opt/homebrew/bin/fish; \
    set -g default-command /opt/homebrew/bin/fish" \
   "set -g default-shell /usr/bin/fish; \
    set -g default-command /usr/bin/fish"
 
 # Enhanced Copy Mode Configuration
 # Use vi-style keys in copy mode
 setw -g mode-keys vi
 
 # Easy copy mode entry with prefix
 # Use v with prefix (Ctrl-s v) instead of [
 bind-key v copy-mode
 # Keep the default [ binding as well
 bind-key [ copy-mode
 
 # Copy mode key bindings (vi-style)
 bind-key -T copy-mode-vi v send-keys -X begin-selection
 bind-key -T copy-mode-vi C-v send-keys -X rectangle-toggle
 # Copy to the system clipboard and exit copy mode, matching Vim yank expectations.
 # OS-specific clipboard command: pbcopy on macOS, xclip on Linux
 if-shell "uname | grep -q Darwin" \
   "bind-key -T copy-mode-vi y send-keys -X copy-pipe-and-cancel 'pbcopy'; \
    bind-key -T copy-mode-vi MouseDragEnd1Pane send-keys -X copy-pipe-and-cancel 'pbcopy'; \
    bind-key -T copy-mode-vi Enter send-keys -X copy-pipe-and-cancel 'pbcopy'" \
   "bind-key -T copy-mode-vi y send-keys -X copy-pipe-and-cancel 'xclip -selection clipboard'; \
    bind-key -T copy-mode-vi MouseDragEnd1Pane send-keys -X copy-pipe-and-cancel 'xclip -selection clipboard'; \
    bind-key -T copy-mode-vi Enter send-keys -X copy-pipe-and-cancel 'xclip -selection clipboard'"
 
 # Clean copy: Y joins lines (fixes copy-paste line breaks from TUI apps like Claude Code)
 # Regular y = raw copy, Y = join lines (for commands), Alt-y = smart (preserve paragraphs)
 # Note: C-y is NOT used here as it conflicts with vi copy-mode scroll-up
 # Note: M-y requires terminal Alt/Meta key support (works in Ghostty/WezTerm/iTerm2).
 #   Y (Shift-y) is the primary binding and works in all terminals.
 # Note: tmux smart mode is a lightweight paragraph-joiner. For full-featured mode
 #   with list/indent/code-fence preservation, use Fish: cfx -p
 if-shell "uname | grep -q Darwin" \
   "bind-key -T copy-mode-vi Y send-keys -X copy-pipe-and-cancel '$HOME/dotfiles/scripts/tmux/tmux-copy-cleanup.sh join'; \
    bind-key -T copy-mode-vi M-y send-keys -X copy-pipe-and-cancel '$HOME/dotfiles/scripts/tmux/tmux-copy-cleanup.sh smart'" \
   "bind-key -T copy-mode-vi Y send-keys -X copy-pipe-and-cancel '$HOME/dotfiles/scripts/tmux/tmux-copy-cleanup.sh join'; \
    bind-key -T copy-mode-vi M-y send-keys -X copy-pipe-and-cancel '$HOME/dotfiles/scripts/tmux/tmux-copy-cleanup.sh smart'"
 
 # Additional convenient bindings for copy mode
 bind-key -T copy-mode-vi Space send-keys -X begin-selection
 bind-key -T copy-mode-vi Escape send-keys -X cancel
 # Match Neovim scroll convention: C-d scrolls up, C-f scrolls down.
 bind-key -T copy-mode-vi C-d send-keys -X halfpage-up
 bind-key -T copy-mode-vi C-f send-keys -X halfpage-down
 bind-key -T copy-mode-vi C-y send-keys -X scroll-up
 bind-key -T copy-mode-vi C-e send-keys -X scroll-down
 # Double-click to select word, triple-click to select line
 bind-key -T copy-mode-vi DoubleClick1Pane select-pane \; send-keys -X select-word
 bind-key -T copy-mode-vi TripleClick1Pane select-pane \; send-keys -X select-line
 
 # Search bindings in copy mode
 bind-key -T copy-mode-vi / command-prompt -i -p "search down" "send -X search-forward-incremental \"%%%\""
 bind-key -T copy-mode-vi ? command-prompt -i -p "search up" "send -X search-backward-incremental \"%%%\""
 
 # Key bindings
 # NOTE: Context-aware 'r' key binding is defined AFTER TPM loads (see bottom of file)
 # to override tmux-powerkit's reload binding
 
 # Window management
 bind c new-window -c "#{pane_current_path}"
 bind % split-window -h -c "#{pane_current_path}"
 bind '"' split-window -v -c "#{pane_current_path}"
 # Pane navigation handled by vim-tmux-navigator (Ctrl-h/j/k/l)
 # Keeping prefix+h/j/k/l as fallback when vim-tmux-navigator is not active
 # bind h select-pane -L
 # bind j select-pane -D
 # bind k select-pane -U
 # bind l select-pane -R
 
 # Smart adaptive splitting - automatically chooses horizontal or vertical based on pane dimensions
 # Uses native tmux format arithmetic (no shell spawn): 8*width vs 20*height accounts for ~2:1 char aspect ratio
 bind -r Space if-shell -F "#{e|<:#{e|*:8,#{pane_width}},#{e|*:20,#{pane_height}}}" \
   "splitw -v -c '#{pane_current_path}' ; display '#[fg=#a6e3a1]↔ Split Horizontal#[default]'" \
   "splitw -h -c '#{pane_current_path}' ; display '#[fg=#89dceb]↕ Split Vertical#[default]'"
 
 # Respawn pane keybinding (restart dead/frozen process)
 bind R respawn-pane -k \; display '#[fg=#a6e3a1] Pane Respawned#[default]'
 
 # M and C-m bindings removed — watcher colors are purely realtime, no manual clear needed
 
 # Toggle logging - save pane output to a file (press again to stop)
 bind L pipe-pane -o "cat >> ~/dotfiles/tmux-logs/\$(date +%Y%m%d-%H%M)-#W.log" \; \
   display '#[fg=#e0af68]📝 Logging: ~/dotfiles/tmux-logs/\$(date +%Y%m%d-%H%M)-#W.log#[default]'
 
 # List of plugins
 set -g @plugin 'tmux-plugins/tpm'
 set -g @plugin 'tmux-plugins/tmux-sensible'
 set -g @plugin 'tmux-plugins/tmux-yank'
 set -g @plugin 'tmux-plugins/tmux-prefix-highlight'
 # set -g @plugin 'alexwforsythe/tmux-which-key'  # Disabled - conflicts with Space key binding
 set -g @plugin 'sainnhe/tmux-fzf'
 set -g @plugin 'tmux-plugins/tmux-open'
 # Configure tmux-open
 set -g @open-S 'https://www.google.com/search?q='
 # Use 'o' to open URLs/files in copy mode
 set -g @open 'o'
 # Use Ctrl-o to open in editor
 set -g @open-editor 'C-o'
 # tmux-copycat removed: deprecated, slow regex on scrollback. Use native / search in copy mode
 set -g @plugin 'tmux-plugins/tmux-pain-control'
 # vim-tmux-navigator: seamless navigation between vim and tmux
 set -g @plugin 'christoomey/vim-tmux-navigator'
 set -g @plugin 'tmux-plugins/tmux-sidebar'
 # tmux-thumbs - fast Rust-based text hints (replacement for tmux-fingers)
 set -g @plugin 'fcsonline/tmux-thumbs'
 set -g @thumbs-key F
 # OS-specific clipboard command for tmux-thumbs
 if-shell "uname | grep -q Darwin" \
   "set -g @thumbs-command 'echo -n {} | pbcopy'" \
   "set -g @thumbs-command 'echo -n {} | xclip -selection clipboard'"
 set -g @thumbs-upcase-command 'open {}'  # Shift+hint opens URL/path
 set -g @thumbs-hint-fg-color yellow
 set -g @thumbs-hint-bg-color black
 set -g @thumbs-select-fg-color yellow
 set -g @thumbs-select-bg-color black
 
 # extrakto - extract text from pane and insert (paths, URLs, IPs, git hashes, etc.)
 # Access with: Ctrl-s + t (text extraction) - overrides default clock-mode
 set -g @plugin 'laktak/extrakto'
 set -g @extrakto_key 't'
 set -g @extrakto_split_size '15'
 set -g @extrakto_popup_size '80%'
 set -g @extrakto_grab_area 'recent'
 # OS-specific clipboard for extrakto
 if-shell "uname | grep -q Darwin" \
   "set -g @extrakto_clip_tool 'pbcopy'; set -g @extrakto_fzf_tool '/opt/homebrew/bin/fzf'" \
   "set -g @extrakto_clip_tool 'xclip -selection clipboard'; set -g @extrakto_fzf_tool 'fzf'"
 
 # tmux-fzf configuration - unified FZF menu for tmux operations
 # Access with: Ctrl-s + f (Find/FZF menu)
 TMUX_FZF_LAUNCH_KEY="f"
 TMUX_FZF_OPTIONS="-p -w 80% -h 80% -m"
 TMUX_FZF_ORDER="session|window|pane|command|keybinding|clipboard"
 
 # tmux-notify - macOS Notification Center alerts when commands finish
 # Access with: Ctrl-s + m (monitor toggle)
 # Works alongside custom tmux-activity-monitor.sh for dual notification
 set -g @plugin 'rickstaa/tmux-notify'
 set -g @tnotify-verbose 'on'
 set -g @tnotify-sleep-duration '5'
 
 # tmux-1password - quick access to 1Password vault items
 # Access with: Ctrl-s + p (password)
 # Requires: brew install --cask 1password-cli && op signin
 set -g @plugin 'yardnsm/tmux-1password'
 set -g @1password-key 'p'
 set -g @1password-copy-to-clipboard 'on'
 set -g @1password-vault ''
 
 # tmux-fuzzback - FZF-powered scrollback buffer search
 # Access with: Ctrl-s + b (buffer search)
 set -g @plugin 'roosta/tmux-fuzzback'
 set -g @fuzzback-bind 'b'
 set -g @fuzzback-popup 1
 set -g @fuzzback-popup-size '80%'
 
 # tmux session persistence across server/device restarts
 set -g @plugin 'tmux-plugins/tmux-resurrect'
 set -g @resurrect-processes 'nvim vim "~ocv->ocv"'
 set -g @resurrect-strategy-nvim 'session'
 set -g @resurrect-strategy-vim 'session'
 set -g @resurrect-capture-pane-contents 'on'
 # Claude Code is intentionally excluded here.
 # OpenCode is restored through the local ocv wrapper.
 
 # tmux-battery removed - was loaded but never displayed (OPTIMIZED)
 set -g @plugin 'tmux-plugins/tmux-cpu'
 # tmux-smooth-scroll removed: every scroll tick spawns bash→bash→perl chain
 # Native tmux scrolling is instant and sufficient
 
 # tmux-powerkit - Ultimate status bar framework with 42 plugins
 set -g @plugin 'fabioluciano/tmux-powerkit'
 set -gu status-right
 
 # Keep tmux-continuum last: it hooks into status-right for autosave.
 set -g @plugin 'tmux-plugins/tmux-continuum'
 set -g @continuum-restore 'on'
 set -g @continuum-save-interval '15'
 
 # tmux-powerkit configuration
 # Two-section layout: ① Session+Windows (left, seamless) | ② Plugins (right)
 
 # Section order and layout
 # 3-element order: session (left) | windows (center) | plugins (right)
 set -g @powerkit_status_order "session,windows,plugins"
 
 # Select plugins to display (comma-separated list)
 # Available: datetime, battery, cpu, memory, disk, load, temperature, fan, gpu, io, brightness, uptime, hostname,
 #           bandwidth, wifi, vpn, latency, public_ip, ssh, weather, volume, now_playing, git, github, gitlab,
 #           bitbucket, jira, kubernetes, terraform, cloud, packages, pomodoro, bitwarden, crypto, stocks, env
 # Default selection: essential system metrics and time
 set -g @powerkit_plugins 'git,datetime'
 
 # Position status bar at bottom (user preference)
 set -g status-position bottom
 
 # Theme configuration - Custom Tokyo Night Storm (editable)
 # To revert to built-in: set theme "tokyo-night" and variant "storm"
 # Edit colors in: scripts/tmux/tokyo-night-custom.sh
 set -g @powerkit_theme "custom"
 set -g @powerkit_custom_theme_path "$HOME/dotfiles/scripts/tmux/tokyo-night-custom.sh"
 
-# Separator style - straight powerline edges, no rounded pills
-set -g @powerkit_separator_style "normal"
-set -g @powerkit_edge_separator_style "normal"
-set -g @powerkit_initial_separator_style "normal"
+# Separator style - no Powerline glyphs; keep status segments rectangular
+set -g @powerkit_separator_style "none"
+set -g @powerkit_edge_separator_style "none"
+set -g @powerkit_initial_separator_style "none"
 
 # Spacing configuration - CRITICAL FIX for gap issue
 set -g @powerkit_elements_spacing "false"     # Remove gaps between sections
 
 # Appearance
 set -g @powerkit_transparent true             # Transparent backgrounds for panes/popup where possible
 set -g @powerkit_update_interval 15           # Git branch + datetime don't need frequent refresh
 
 # Plugin-specific configurations
 set -g @powerkit_datetime_format "%H:%M %a %d %b"  # Time and date format
 set -g @powerkit_git_show_branch true         # Show current Git branch
 set -g @powerkit_git_show_status true         # Show Git status indicators (*, +, etc.)
 set -g @powerkit_bandwidth_interface "auto"   # Auto-detect network interface
 set -g @powerkit_wifi_show_signal true        # Show WiFi signal strength
 
 # Catppuccin Mocha Theme with omerxx-inspired aesthetics
 
 # Session Management - Single unified manager with tmuxinator integration
 # Access with: Ctrl-s + S
 bind S display-popup -E -h 80% -w 80% "$HOME/dotfiles/scripts/tmux/tmux-session-manager.sh"
 
 # Session Trash Recovery - Browse and restore killed sessions
 # Access with: Ctrl-s + T
 bind T display-popup -E -h 80% -w 80% "$HOME/dotfiles/scripts/tmux/tmux-session-trash.sh restore"
 
 # Which-key configuration (disabled - conflicts with Space binding)
 # set -g @which-key-popup 1
 
 # =============================================================================
 # STATUS BAR CONFIGURATION - Three Section Layout
 # =============================================================================
 # ① Session (left) | ② Plugins (center) | ③ Windows (right/center)
 # Powered by tmux-powerkit with straight-edged segments
 # =============================================================================
 
 # Status bar layout configuration
 set -g status-justify centre              # Center window list
 set -g status-left-length 50              # Space for session indicator
 set -g status-right-length 150            # Space for plugin pills
 
 # Background styling (powerkit themes will override with their colors)
 set -g status-style "bg=#1e1e2e,fg=#cdd6f4"
 
 # Ensure pane backgrounds are not forced to a solid color
 # (lets terminal/wezterm transparency show through and enables nvim winblend)
 set -g window-style 'bg=default'
 set -g window-active-style 'bg=default'
 set -g popup-style 'bg=default,fg=default'
 
 # Window status styling (powerkit may override, but provides fallback)
 # Active window - highlighted fallback when powerkit is unavailable
-setw -g window-status-current-style "fg=#1e1e2e,bg=#73daca,bold"
+setw -g window-status-current-style "fg=#c0caf5,bg=#2f3549,bold"
 # Inactive windows - subdued appearance
 setw -g window-status-style "fg=#6c7086,bg=#313244"
 
 # Pane borders - subtle with accent on active
 set -g pane-border-style "fg=#313244"
 set -g pane-active-border-style "fg=#cba6f7"
 
 # Message style
 set -g message-style "bg=#313244,fg=#cdd6f4"
 set -g message-command-style "bg=#313244,fg=#cdd6f4"
 
 # Copy mode colors
 set -g mode-style "bg=#45475a,fg=#cdd6f4"
 
 # # Update status bar every 30 seconds for CPU/RAM (OPTIMIZED: reduced from 10s)
 # # This reduces refresh overhead by 3x while still providing timely updates
 # set -g status-interval 30
 
 # Auto-install TPM if not present
 if "test ! -d ~/.tmux/plugins/tpm" \
    "run 'git clone https://github.com/tmux-plugins/tpm ~/.tmux/plugins/tpm && ~/.tmux/plugins/tpm/bin/install_plugins'"
 
 # Automatically split new windows and run claude (DISABLED)
 # This creates a vertical split with claude in the right pane for every new window
 # set-hook -g after-new-window 'split-window -h -p 40 "claude"'
 
 # Manual keybinding to open Claude Code when needed
 # Prefix + C opens Claude in a vertical split (40% width on the right)
 bind C split-window -h -p 40 "claude --dangerously-skip-permissions"
 
 # Quick launch: Ctrl-s a opens Claude with smart adaptive split
 bind a if-shell -F "#{e|<:#{e|*:8,#{pane_width}},#{e|*:20,#{pane_height}}}" \
   "splitw -v -c '#{pane_current_path}' 'claude --dangerously-skip-permissions --effort max --remote-control' ; display '#[fg=#a6e3a1]↔ Split Horizontal (Claude)#[default]'" \
   "splitw -h -c '#{pane_current_path}' 'claude --dangerously-skip-permissions --effort max --remote-control' ; display '#[fg=#89dceb]↕ Split Vertical (Claude)#[default]'"
 
 # Quick launch: Ctrl-s O opens OpenCode in its allow-permissions setup
 bind O if-shell -F "#{e|<:#{e|*:8,#{pane_width}},#{e|*:20,#{pane_height}}}" \
   "splitw -v -c '#{pane_current_path}' '$HOME/dotfiles/scripts/opencode/tmux-open.sh' ; display '#[fg=#a6e3a1]↔ Split Horizontal (OpenCode)#[default]'" \
   "splitw -h -c '#{pane_current_path}' '$HOME/dotfiles/scripts/opencode/tmux-open.sh' ; display '#[fg=#89dceb]↕ Split Vertical (OpenCode)#[default]'"
 
 # Quick launch: Ctrl-s e opens Neovim in current directory
 # C-c first to normalize Fish vi-mode state (lands us in insert mode at empty
 # prompt) so the typed `nvim .` isn't eaten as vi-mode normal commands.
 bind e send-keys C-c "nvim ." Enter
 
 # Quick launch: Ctrl-s E opens per-directory gwtt-prompt.local.md with smart adaptive split
 bind E if-shell -F "#{e|<:#{e|*:8,#{pane_width}},#{e|*:20,#{pane_height}}}" \
   "splitw -v -c '#{pane_current_path}' '$HOME/dotfiles/scripts/tmux/gwtt-prompt-edit.sh' ; display '#[fg=#a6e3a1]↔ Split Horizontal (gwtt-prompt)#[default]'" \
   "splitw -h -c '#{pane_current_path}' '$HOME/dotfiles/scripts/tmux/gwtt-prompt-edit.sh' ; display '#[fg=#89dceb]↕ Split Vertical (gwtt-prompt)#[default]'"
 
 # URL handler binding (smart: tries visible pane first, falls back to full scrollback)
 bind-key o run-shell -b "$HOME/dotfiles/scripts/tmux/tmux-url-handler.sh"
 
 # Keep default tmux bindings for navigation
 # Prefix + O: select previous pane
 # Prefix + p: previous window (default tmux behavior)
 
 # Initialize TMUX plugin manager (keep this line at the very bottom of tmux.conf)
 run '~/.tmux/plugins/tpm/tpm'
 
 # Force tmux-open bindings after all plugins load
 run-shell '~/.tmux/plugins/tmux-open/open.tmux'
 
 # Restore prefix+x to kill-pane (extrakto plugin overrides this even with @extrakto_key changed)
 # Enhanced: capture window info before killing for worktree auto-cleanup.
 # Run the wrapper in tmux background so pane close stays responsive.
 bind-key x run-shell -b '~/dotfiles/scripts/tmux/tmux-kill-pane-cleanup.sh "#{session_name}" "#{window_name}" "#{window_panes}"'
 
 # Kill window: prefix + X (Shift-x) - closes entire window with worktree cleanup
 bind-key X confirm-before -p 'Kill window "#{window_name}"? (y/n)' \
   "run-shell -b '~/dotfiles/scripts/tmux/tmux-worktree-cleanup.sh \"#{session_name}\" \"#{window_name}\"'; kill-window"
 
 # Force status bar position and layout (override plugin defaults)
 set -g status-position bottom
 set -g status-justify centre
 
 # Final overrides to keep transparent panes/popups after plugins load
 set -g window-style 'bg=default'
 set -g window-active-style 'bg=default'
 set -g popup-style 'bg=default,fg=default'
 
 # Override powerkit's default 5s status-interval (must be after TPM)
 set -g status-interval 15
 
 # Fix nested fish: reset default-command to empty after TPM loads.
 # tmux-sensible (loaded by TPM) overrides empty default-command with
 # "reattach-to-user-namespace -l $SHELL". By setting it to fish BEFORE TPM,
 # sensible sees non-empty value and leaves it alone. Now we reset to empty
 # so tmux launches default-shell directly (no "fish -c fish" nesting).
 set -g default-command ''
 
 # Reassert clipboard copy-mode bindings after TPM plugins load.
 # tmux-yank can override these with legacy reattach-to-user-namespace commands.
 if-shell "uname | grep -q Darwin" \
   "bind-key -T copy-mode-vi y send-keys -X copy-pipe-and-cancel 'pbcopy'; \
    bind-key -T copy-mode-vi MouseDragEnd1Pane send-keys -X copy-pipe-and-cancel 'pbcopy'; \
    bind-key -T copy-mode-vi Enter send-keys -X copy-pipe-and-cancel 'pbcopy'" \
   "bind-key -T copy-mode-vi y send-keys -X copy-pipe-and-cancel 'xclip -selection clipboard'; \
    bind-key -T copy-mode-vi MouseDragEnd1Pane send-keys -X copy-pipe-and-cancel 'xclip -selection clipboard'; \
    bind-key -T copy-mode-vi Enter send-keys -X copy-pipe-and-cancel 'xclip -selection clipboard'"
 
 # Inject @wname_style into status bar window tabs (must be after TPM/powerkit)
 # Makes agent state colors visible in the status bar, not just choose-tree
 run-shell '~/dotfiles/scripts/tmux/inject-wname-style.sh'
 
 # Colored choose-tree session/window picker (must be after TPM)
 # Tree labels (session/window names, markers) use pane fg — set via select-pane -P
 # pane-mode-changed hook resets pane style when exiting choose-tree
 # Agent colors set by watcher via @wname_style: running=green, idle=yellow, stuck=red
 set-hook -g pane-mode-changed[100] 'if -F "#{pane_in_mode}" "" "select-pane -P default"'
 bind s select-pane -P 'fg=#9ece6a' \; choose-tree -Zw -F '#{?pane_format,#[fg=#7dcfff]#{pane_current_command} #[fg=#565f89](#{pane_current_path}),#{?window_format,#{@wname_style}#{window_name}#{?window_active, *,},#[fg=#565f89]#{session_windows} windows#{?session_attached, #[fg=#e0af68](attached),}}}'
 bind w select-pane -P 'fg=#9ece6a' \; choose-tree -Zw -F '#{?pane_format,#[fg=#7dcfff]#{pane_current_command} #[fg=#565f89](#{pane_current_path}),#{?window_format,#{@wname_style}#{window_name}#{?window_active, *,},#[fg=#565f89]#{session_windows} windows#{?session_attached, #[fg=#e0af68](attached),}}}'
 
 # Context-aware r key: recall when in Claude, config reload otherwise
 # Must be after TPM to override tmux-powerkit's reload binding
 bind r if-shell "ps -o args= -t #{pane_tty} 2>/dev/null | grep -q 'claude'" \
     "confirm-before -p 'Kill Claude pane and run recall? (y/n)' 'respawn-pane -k \"/opt/homebrew/bin/recall\"'" \
     "source-file ~/.tmux.conf \\; display '#[fg=#a6e3a1]󰑓 Config Reloaded#[default]'"

parts.text:
Adjusted.

Now:
- No curved or diamond Powerline edges: `@powerkit_separator_style`, `@powerkit_edge_separator_style`, and `@powerkit_initial_separator_style` are all `none`.
- Colors are darker and more muted:
  - Near-black status background
  - Dark blue-grey active window
  - Darker inactive windows
  - Muted prefix/copy/git/datetime colors

Validated and reloaded tmux successfully with `tmux source-file "$HOME/.tmux.conf"`.

diffs.patch:
Index: .tmux.conf
===================================================================
--- .tmux.conf	
+++ .tmux.conf	
@@ -1,464 +1,470 @@
 # Basic tmux configuration
 set -g default-terminal "tmux-256color"
 set -ga terminal-overrides ",*:Tc:RGB,xterm-256color:Ms=\\E]52;c;%p2%s\\7,tmux-256color:Ms=\\E]52;c;%p2%s\\7"
 
 # Set prefix to Ctrl-s (more ergonomic than Ctrl-Space)
 set -g prefix C-s
 unbind C-b
 unbind C-a
 # Unbind C-z to ensure it's passed through to nested applications (like Neovim terminal)
 unbind C-z
 # Double tap Ctrl-s to send literal Ctrl-s to applications
 bind-key C-s send-prefix
 
 # Enable key repeat
 set -g repeat-time 0
 
 # Basic settings
 set -g mouse on
 set -g escape-time 0
 set -g focus-events on
 set -g history-limit 100000
 set -g base-index 1
 setw -g pane-base-index 1
 set -g renumber-windows on
 setw -g automatic-rename off
 
 # Prevent Claude Code nested-session false positives in tmux
 set-environment -gr CLAUDECODE
 
 # When a session is destroyed, switch to the most recently active session
 # instead of detaching the client (which would close the terminal).
 # Tested with tmux 3.5a.
 set -g detach-on-destroy off
 
 # Proactively ensure "main" session exists at server start.
 # Combined with the session-closed hook below, this guarantees a fallback
 # target is always available for detach-on-destroy.
 # exit-empty is set to off so the server survives the brief window between
 # sole-session destruction and hook recreation.
 set -g exit-empty off
 run-shell 'tmux has-session -t main 2>/dev/null || tmux new-session -d -s main'
 
 # Reactively recreate "main" if destroyed while the server is still running.
 set-hook -g session-closed 'if-shell "! tmux has-session -t main 2>/dev/null" "new-session -d -s main"'
 
 # Activity monitoring - custom completion detection
 # Monitors activity, then checks for completion after silence period
 setw -g monitor-activity on
 set -g visual-activity off  # Don't show visual alerts
 set -g activity-action any  # Trigger on ALL windows including current (enables Claude highlighting)
 # Disable visual styling for windows with activity (no grey background)
 set -g window-status-activity-style "fg=#6c7086,bg=#1e1e2e"
 
 # Disable silence monitoring (alert-silence hook doesn't fire reliably)
 setw -g monitor-silence 0
 set -g visual-silence off
 
 # Agent activity monitoring:
 #   Claude: event-driven via scripts/bin/claude wrapper (injects --settings hooks)
 #   Codex/Opencode: polling fallback via tmux-claude-watcher.sh daemon (10s interval)
 # Explicitly unset stale hooks from earlier config versions
 set-hook -gu alert-activity
 set-hook -gu after-select-window
 
 # Start watcher daemon for codex/opencode (claude is handled by the wrapper)
 run-shell '~/dotfiles/scripts/tmux/tmux-claude-watcher.sh start >/dev/null 2>&1 || true'
 
 # Bell on activity completion - disabled
 set -g bell-action none
 set -g visual-bell off  # Disable visual bell flash
 
 # Clipboard integration
 set -g set-clipboard on
 # Allow OSC-52 escape sequences to pass through to the terminal
 # Required for Neovim clipboard to work in SSH/container sessions
 set -g allow-passthrough on
 
 # OS-specific default shell configuration
 # reattach-to-user-namespace removed: not needed since macOS 12 (Monterey)
 # pbcopy/pbpaste and OSC-52 work natively in tmux on modern macOS
 # NOTE: default-command is set here to block tmux-sensible from overriding it
 # with "reattach-to-user-namespace -l $SHELL" (which spawns zsh, not fish).
 # After TPM loads, default-command is reset to empty (see post-TPM section)
 # so tmux launches default-shell directly without nesting.
 if-shell "uname | grep -q Darwin" \
   "set -g default-shell /opt/homebrew/bin/fish; \
    set -g default-command /opt/homebrew/bin/fish" \
   "set -g default-shell /usr/bin/fish; \
    set -g default-command /usr/bin/fish"
 
 # Enhanced Copy Mode Configuration
 # Use vi-style keys in copy mode
 setw -g mode-keys vi
 
 # Easy copy mode entry with prefix
 # Use v with prefix (Ctrl-s v) instead of [
 bind-key v copy-mode
 # Keep the default [ binding as well
 bind-key [ copy-mode
 
 # Copy mode key bindings (vi-style)
 bind-key -T copy-mode-vi v send-keys -X begin-selection
 bind-key -T copy-mode-vi C-v send-keys -X rectangle-toggle
 # Copy to the system clipboard and exit copy mode, matching Vim yank expectations.
 # OS-specific clipboard command: pbcopy on macOS, xclip on Linux
 if-shell "uname | grep -q Darwin" \
   "bind-key -T copy-mode-vi y send-keys -X copy-pipe-and-cancel 'pbcopy'; \
    bind-key -T copy-mode-vi MouseDragEnd1Pane send-keys -X copy-pipe-and-cancel 'pbcopy'; \
    bind-key -T copy-mode-vi Enter send-keys -X copy-pipe-and-cancel 'pbcopy'" \
   "bind-key -T copy-mode-vi y send-keys -X copy-pipe-and-cancel 'xclip -selection clipboard'; \
    bind-key -T copy-mode-vi MouseDragEnd1Pane send-keys -X copy-pipe-and-cancel 'xclip -selection clipboard'; \
    bind-key -T copy-mode-vi Enter send-keys -X copy-pipe-and-cancel 'xclip -selection clipboard'"
 
 # Clean copy: Y joins lines (fixes copy-paste line breaks from TUI apps like Claude Code)
 # Regular y = raw copy, Y = join lines (for commands), Alt-y = smart (preserve paragraphs)
 # Note: C-y is NOT used here as it conflicts with vi copy-mode scroll-up
 # Note: M-y requires terminal Alt/Meta key support (works in Ghostty/WezTerm/iTerm2).
 #   Y (Shift-y) is the primary binding and works in all terminals.
 # Note: tmux smart mode is a lightweight paragraph-joiner. For full-featured mode
 #   with list/indent/code-fence preservation, use Fish: cfx -p
 if-shell "uname | grep -q Darwin" \
   "bind-key -T copy-mode-vi Y send-keys -X copy-pipe-and-cancel '$HOME/dotfiles/scripts/tmux/tmux-copy-cleanup.sh join'; \
    bind-key -T copy-mode-vi M-y send-keys -X copy-pipe-and-cancel '$HOME/dotfiles/scripts/tmux/tmux-copy-cleanup.sh smart'" \
   "bind-key -T copy-mode-vi Y send-keys -X copy-pipe-and-cancel '$HOME/dotfiles/scripts/tmux/tmux-copy-cleanup.sh join'; \
    bind-key -T copy-mode-vi M-y send-keys -X copy-pipe-and-cancel '$HOME/dotfiles/scripts/tmux/tmux-copy-cleanup.sh smart'"
 
 # Additional convenient bindings for copy mode
 bind-key -T copy-mode-vi Space send-keys -X begin-selection
 bind-key -T copy-mode-vi Escape send-keys -X cancel
 # Match Neovim scroll convention: C-d scrolls up, C-f scrolls down.
 bind-key -T copy-mode-vi C-d send-keys -X halfpage-up
 bind-key -T copy-mode-vi C-f send-keys -X halfpage-down
 bind-key -T copy-mode-vi C-y send-keys -X scroll-up
 bind-key -T copy-mode-vi C-e send-keys -X scroll-down
 # Double-click to select word, triple-click to select line
 bind-key -T copy-mode-vi DoubleClick1Pane select-pane \; send-keys -X select-word
 bind-key -T copy-mode-vi TripleClick1Pane select-pane \; send-keys -X select-line
 
 # Search bindings in copy mode
 bind-key -T copy-mode-vi / command-prompt -i -p "search down" "send -X search-forward-incremental \"%%%\""
 bind-key -T copy-mode-vi ? command-prompt -i -p "search up" "send -X search-backward-incremental \"%%%\""
 
 # Key bindings
 # NOTE: Context-aware 'r' key binding is defined AFTER TPM loads (see bottom of file)
 # to override tmux-powerkit's reload binding
 
 # Window management
 bind c new-window -c "#{pane_current_path}"
 bind % split-window -h -c "#{pane_current_path}"
 bind '"' split-window -v -c "#{pane_current_path}"
 # Pane navigation handled by vim-tmux-navigator (Ctrl-h/j/k/l)
 # Keeping prefix+h/j/k/l as fallback when vim-tmux-navigator is not active
 # bind h select-pane -L
 # bind j select-pane -D
 # bind k select-pane -U
 # bind l select-pane -R
 
 # Smart adaptive splitting - automatically chooses horizontal or vertical based on pane dimensions
 # Uses native tmux format arithmetic (no shell spawn): 8*width vs 20*height accounts for ~2:1 char aspect ratio
 bind -r Space if-shell -F "#{e|<:#{e|*:8,#{pane_width}},#{e|*:20,#{pane_height}}}" \
   "splitw -v -c '#{pane_current_path}' ; display '#[fg=#a6e3a1]↔ Split Horizontal#[default]'" \
   "splitw -h -c '#{pane_current_path}' ; display '#[fg=#89dceb]↕ Split Vertical#[default]'"
 
 # Respawn pane keybinding (restart dead/frozen process)
 bind R respawn-pane -k \; display '#[fg=#a6e3a1] Pane Respawned#[default]'
 
 # M and C-m bindings removed — watcher colors are purely realtime, no manual clear needed
 
 # Toggle logging - save pane output to a file (press again to stop)
 bind L pipe-pane -o "cat >> ~/dotfiles/tmux-logs/\$(date +%Y%m%d-%H%M)-#W.log" \; \
   display '#[fg=#e0af68]📝 Logging: ~/dotfiles/tmux-logs/\$(date +%Y%m%d-%H%M)-#W.log#[default]'
 
 # List of plugins
 set -g @plugin 'tmux-plugins/tpm'
 set -g @plugin 'tmux-plugins/tmux-sensible'
 set -g @plugin 'tmux-plugins/tmux-yank'
 set -g @plugin 'tmux-plugins/tmux-prefix-highlight'
 # set -g @plugin 'alexwforsythe/tmux-which-key'  # Disabled - conflicts with Space key binding
 set -g @plugin 'sainnhe/tmux-fzf'
 set -g @plugin 'tmux-plugins/tmux-open'
 # Configure tmux-open
 set -g @open-S 'https://www.google.com/search?q='
 # Use 'o' to open URLs/files in copy mode
 set -g @open 'o'
 # Use Ctrl-o to open in editor
 set -g @open-editor 'C-o'
 # tmux-copycat removed: deprecated, slow regex on scrollback. Use native / search in copy mode
 set -g @plugin 'tmux-plugins/tmux-pain-control'
 # vim-tmux-navigator: seamless navigation between vim and tmux
 set -g @plugin 'christoomey/vim-tmux-navigator'
 set -g @plugin 'tmux-plugins/tmux-sidebar'
 # tmux-thumbs - fast Rust-based text hints (replacement for tmux-fingers)
 set -g @plugin 'fcsonline/tmux-thumbs'
 set -g @thumbs-key F
 # OS-specific clipboard command for tmux-thumbs
 if-shell "uname | grep -q Darwin" \
   "set -g @thumbs-command 'echo -n {} | pbcopy'" \
   "set -g @thumbs-command 'echo -n {} | xclip -selection clipboard'"
 set -g @thumbs-upcase-command 'open {}'  # Shift+hint opens URL/path
 set -g @thumbs-hint-fg-color yellow
 set -g @thumbs-hint-bg-color black
 set -g @thumbs-select-fg-color yellow
 set -g @thumbs-select-bg-color black
 
 # extrakto - extract text from pane and insert (paths, URLs, IPs, git hashes, etc.)
 # Access with: Ctrl-s + t (text extraction) - overrides default clock-mode
 set -g @plugin 'laktak/extrakto'
 set -g @extrakto_key 't'
 set -g @extrakto_split_size '15'
 set -g @extrakto_popup_size '80%'
 set -g @extrakto_grab_area 'recent'
 # OS-specific clipboard for extrakto
 if-shell "uname | grep -q Darwin" \
   "set -g @extrakto_clip_tool 'pbcopy'; set -g @extrakto_fzf_tool '/opt/homebrew/bin/fzf'" \
   "set -g @extrakto_clip_tool 'xclip -selection clipboard'; set -g @extrakto_fzf_tool 'fzf'"
 
 # tmux-fzf configuration - unified FZF menu for tmux operations
 # Access with: Ctrl-s + f (Find/FZF menu)
 TMUX_FZF_LAUNCH_KEY="f"
 TMUX_FZF_OPTIONS="-p -w 80% -h 80% -m"
 TMUX_FZF_ORDER="session|window|pane|command|keybinding|clipboard"
 
 # tmux-notify - macOS Notification Center alerts when commands finish
 # Access with: Ctrl-s + m (monitor toggle)
 # Works alongside custom tmux-activity-monitor.sh for dual notification
 set -g @plugin 'rickstaa/tmux-notify'
 set -g @tnotify-verbose 'on'
 set -g @tnotify-sleep-duration '5'
 
 # tmux-1password - quick access to 1Password vault items
 # Access with: Ctrl-s + p (password)
 # Requires: brew install --cask 1password-cli && op signin
 set -g @plugin 'yardnsm/tmux-1password'
 set -g @1password-key 'p'
 set -g @1password-copy-to-clipboard 'on'
 set -g @1password-vault ''
 
 # tmux-fuzzback - FZF-powered scrollback buffer search
 # Access with: Ctrl-s + b (buffer search)
 set -g @plugin 'roosta/tmux-fuzzback'
 set -g @fuzzback-bind 'b'
 set -g @fuzzback-popup 1
 set -g @fuzzback-popup-size '80%'
 
 # tmux session persistence across server/device restarts
 set -g @plugin 'tmux-plugins/tmux-resurrect'
 set -g @resurrect-processes 'nvim vim "~ocv->ocv"'
 set -g @resurrect-strategy-nvim 'session'
 set -g @resurrect-strategy-vim 'session'
 set -g @resurrect-capture-pane-contents 'on'
 # Claude Code is intentionally excluded here.
 # OpenCode is restored through the local ocv wrapper.
 
 # tmux-battery removed - was loaded but never displayed (OPTIMIZED)
 set -g @plugin 'tmux-plugins/tmux-cpu'
 # tmux-smooth-scroll removed: every scroll tick spawns bash→bash→perl chain
 # Native tmux scrolling is instant and sufficient
 
 # tmux-powerkit - Ultimate status bar framework with 42 plugins
 set -g @plugin 'fabioluciano/tmux-powerkit'
 set -gu status-right
 
 # Keep tmux-continuum last: it hooks into status-right for autosave.
 set -g @plugin 'tmux-plugins/tmux-continuum'
 set -g @continuum-restore 'on'
 set -g @continuum-save-interval '15'
 
 # tmux-powerkit configuration
 # Two-section layout: ① Session+Windows (left, seamless) | ② Plugins (right)
 
 # Section order and layout
 # 3-element order: session (left) | windows (center) | plugins (right)
 set -g @powerkit_status_order "session,windows,plugins"
 
 # Select plugins to display (comma-separated list)
 # Available: datetime, battery, cpu, memory, disk, load, temperature, fan, gpu, io, brightness, uptime, hostname,
 #           bandwidth, wifi, vpn, latency, public_ip, ssh, weather, volume, now_playing, git, github, gitlab,
 #           bitbucket, jira, kubernetes, terraform, cloud, packages, pomodoro, bitwarden, crypto, stocks, env
 # Default selection: essential system metrics and time
 set -g @powerkit_plugins 'git,datetime'
 
 # Position status bar at bottom (user preference)
 set -g status-position bottom
+set -g @powerkit_status_position "bottom"
+set -g @powerkit_status_interval 15
 
 # Theme configuration - Custom Tokyo Night Storm (editable)
 # To revert to built-in: set theme "tokyo-night" and variant "storm"
 # Edit colors in: scripts/tmux/tokyo-night-custom.sh
 set -g @powerkit_theme "custom"
 set -g @powerkit_custom_theme_path "$HOME/dotfiles/scripts/tmux/tokyo-night-custom.sh"
 
 # Separator style - no Powerline glyphs; keep status segments rectangular
 set -g @powerkit_separator_style "none"
 set -g @powerkit_edge_separator_style "none"
 set -g @powerkit_initial_separator_style "none"
 
 # Spacing configuration - CRITICAL FIX for gap issue
 set -g @powerkit_elements_spacing "false"     # Remove gaps between sections
 
 # Appearance
 set -g @powerkit_transparent true             # Transparent backgrounds for panes/popup where possible
 set -g @powerkit_update_interval 15           # Git branch + datetime don't need frequent refresh
 
 # Plugin-specific configurations
 set -g @powerkit_datetime_format "%H:%M %a %d %b"  # Time and date format
 set -g @powerkit_git_show_branch true         # Show current Git branch
 set -g @powerkit_git_show_status true         # Show Git status indicators (*, +, etc.)
 set -g @powerkit_bandwidth_interface "auto"   # Auto-detect network interface
 set -g @powerkit_wifi_show_signal true        # Show WiFi signal strength
 
 # Catppuccin Mocha Theme with omerxx-inspired aesthetics
 
 # Session Management - Single unified manager with tmuxinator integration
 # Access with: Ctrl-s + S
 bind S display-popup -E -h 80% -w 80% "$HOME/dotfiles/scripts/tmux/tmux-session-manager.sh"
 
 # Session Trash Recovery - Browse and restore killed sessions
 # Access with: Ctrl-s + T
 bind T display-popup -E -h 80% -w 80% "$HOME/dotfiles/scripts/tmux/tmux-session-trash.sh restore"
 
 # Which-key configuration (disabled - conflicts with Space binding)
 # set -g @which-key-popup 1
 
 # =============================================================================
 # STATUS BAR CONFIGURATION - Three Section Layout
 # =============================================================================
 # ① Session (left) | ② Plugins (center) | ③ Windows (right/center)
 # Powered by tmux-powerkit with straight-edged segments
 # =============================================================================
 
 # Status bar layout configuration
 set -g status-justify centre              # Center window list
 set -g status-left-length 50              # Space for session indicator
 set -g status-right-length 150            # Space for plugin pills
 
 # Background styling (powerkit themes will override with their colors)
 set -g status-style "bg=#1e1e2e,fg=#cdd6f4"
 
 # Ensure pane backgrounds are not forced to a solid color
 # (lets terminal/wezterm transparency show through and enables nvim winblend)
 set -g window-style 'bg=default'
 set -g window-active-style 'bg=default'
 set -g popup-style 'bg=default,fg=default'
 
 # Window status styling (powerkit may override, but provides fallback)
 # Active window - highlighted fallback when powerkit is unavailable
 setw -g window-status-current-style "fg=#c0caf5,bg=#2f3549,bold"
 # Inactive windows - subdued appearance
 setw -g window-status-style "fg=#6c7086,bg=#313244"
 
 # Pane borders - subtle with accent on active
 set -g pane-border-style "fg=#313244"
 set -g pane-active-border-style "fg=#cba6f7"
 
 # Message style
 set -g message-style "bg=#313244,fg=#cdd6f4"
 set -g message-command-style "bg=#313244,fg=#cdd6f4"
 
 # Copy mode colors
 set -g mode-style "bg=#45475a,fg=#cdd6f4"
 
 # # Update status bar every 30 seconds for CPU/RAM (OPTIMIZED: reduced from 10s)
 # # This reduces refresh overhead by 3x while still providing timely updates
 # set -g status-interval 30
 
 # Auto-install TPM if not present
 if "test ! -d ~/.tmux/plugins/tpm" \
    "run 'git clone https://github.com/tmux-plugins/tpm ~/.tmux/plugins/tpm && ~/.tmux/plugins/tpm/bin/install_plugins'"
 
 # Automatically split new windows and run claude (DISABLED)
 # This creates a vertical split with claude in the right pane for every new window
 # set-hook -g after-new-window 'split-window -h -p 40 "claude"'
 
 # Manual keybinding to open Claude Code when needed
 # Prefix + C opens Claude in a vertical split (40% width on the right)
 bind C split-window -h -p 40 "claude --dangerously-skip-permissions"
 
 # Quick launch: Ctrl-s a opens Claude with smart adaptive split
 bind a if-shell -F "#{e|<:#{e|*:8,#{pane_width}},#{e|*:20,#{pane_height}}}" \
   "splitw -v -c '#{pane_current_path}' 'claude --dangerously-skip-permissions --effort max --remote-control' ; display '#[fg=#a6e3a1]↔ Split Horizontal (Claude)#[default]'" \
   "splitw -h -c '#{pane_current_path}' 'claude --dangerously-skip-permissions --effort max --remote-control' ; display '#[fg=#89dceb]↕ Split Vertical (Claude)#[default]'"
 
 # Quick launch: Ctrl-s O opens OpenCode in its allow-permissions setup
 bind O if-shell -F "#{e|<:#{e|*:8,#{pane_width}},#{e|*:20,#{pane_height}}}" \
   "splitw -v -c '#{pane_current_path}' '$HOME/dotfiles/scripts/opencode/tmux-open.sh' ; display '#[fg=#a6e3a1]↔ Split Horizontal (OpenCode)#[default]'" \
   "splitw -h -c '#{pane_current_path}' '$HOME/dotfiles/scripts/opencode/tmux-open.sh' ; display '#[fg=#89dceb]↕ Split Vertical (OpenCode)#[default]'"
 
 # Quick launch: Ctrl-s e opens Neovim in current directory
 # C-c first to normalize Fish vi-mode state (lands us in insert mode at empty
 # prompt) so the typed `nvim .` isn't eaten as vi-mode normal commands.
 bind e send-keys C-c "nvim ." Enter
 
 # Quick launch: Ctrl-s E opens per-directory gwtt-prompt.local.md with smart adaptive split
 bind E if-shell -F "#{e|<:#{e|*:8,#{pane_width}},#{e|*:20,#{pane_height}}}" \
   "splitw -v -c '#{pane_current_path}' '$HOME/dotfiles/scripts/tmux/gwtt-prompt-edit.sh' ; display '#[fg=#a6e3a1]↔ Split Horizontal (gwtt-prompt)#[default]'" \
   "splitw -h -c '#{pane_current_path}' '$HOME/dotfiles/scripts/tmux/gwtt-prompt-edit.sh' ; display '#[fg=#89dceb]↕ Split Vertical (gwtt-prompt)#[default]'"
 
 # URL handler binding (smart: tries visible pane first, falls back to full scrollback)
 bind-key o run-shell -b "$HOME/dotfiles/scripts/tmux/tmux-url-handler.sh"
 
 # Keep default tmux bindings for navigation
 # Prefix + O: select previous pane
 # Prefix + p: previous window (default tmux behavior)
 
 # Initialize TMUX plugin manager (keep this line at the very bottom of tmux.conf)
 run '~/.tmux/plugins/tpm/tpm'
 
 # Force tmux-open bindings after all plugins load
 run-shell '~/.tmux/plugins/tmux-open/open.tmux'
 
 # Restore prefix+x to kill-pane (extrakto plugin overrides this even with @extrakto_key changed)
 # Enhanced: capture window info before killing for worktree auto-cleanup.
 # Run the wrapper in tmux background so pane close stays responsive.
 bind-key x run-shell -b '~/dotfiles/scripts/tmux/tmux-kill-pane-cleanup.sh "#{session_name}" "#{window_name}" "#{window_panes}"'
 
 # Kill window: prefix + X (Shift-x) - closes entire window with worktree cleanup
 bind-key X confirm-before -p 'Kill window "#{window_name}"? (y/n)' \
   "run-shell -b '~/dotfiles/scripts/tmux/tmux-worktree-cleanup.sh \"#{session_name}\" \"#{window_name}\"'; kill-window"
 
 # Force status bar position and layout (override plugin defaults)
 set -g status-position bottom
 set -g status-justify centre
 
 # Final overrides to keep transparent panes/popups after plugins load
 set -g window-style 'bg=default'
 set -g window-active-style 'bg=default'
 set -g popup-style 'bg=default,fg=default'
 
 # Override powerkit's default 5s status-interval (must be after TPM)
 set -g status-interval 15
 
+# PowerKit's renderer only batch-loads @powerkit_* options when TMUX is set.
+# External `tmux source-file` reloads can otherwise render the default theme.
+run-shell -b 'TMUX_SOCKET=$(tmux display-message -p "#{socket_path}" 2>/dev/null); TMUX_PID=$(tmux display-message -p "#{pid}" 2>/dev/null); TMUX_SESSION=$(tmux display-message -p "#{session_id}" 2>/dev/null); TMUX="$TMUX_SOCKET,$TMUX_PID,$TMUX_SESSION" POWERKIT_ROOT="$HOME/.tmux/plugins/tmux-powerkit" bash "$HOME/.tmux/plugins/tmux-powerkit/src/helpers/reload_theme.sh" >/dev/null 2>&1 || true'
+
 # Fix nested fish: reset default-command to empty after TPM loads.
 # tmux-sensible (loaded by TPM) overrides empty default-command with
 # "reattach-to-user-namespace -l $SHELL". By setting it to fish BEFORE TPM,
 # sensible sees non-empty value and leaves it alone. Now we reset to empty
 # so tmux launches default-shell directly (no "fish -c fish" nesting).
 set -g default-command ''
 
 # Reassert clipboard copy-mode bindings after TPM plugins load.
 # tmux-yank can override these with legacy reattach-to-user-namespace commands.
 if-shell "uname | grep -q Darwin" \
   "bind-key -T copy-mode-vi y send-keys -X copy-pipe-and-cancel 'pbcopy'; \
    bind-key -T copy-mode-vi MouseDragEnd1Pane send-keys -X copy-pipe-and-cancel 'pbcopy'; \
    bind-key -T copy-mode-vi Enter send-keys -X copy-pipe-and-cancel 'pbcopy'" \
   "bind-key -T copy-mode-vi y send-keys -X copy-pipe-and-cancel 'xclip -selection clipboard'; \
    bind-key -T copy-mode-vi MouseDragEnd1Pane send-keys -X copy-pipe-and-cancel 'xclip -selection clipboard'; \
    bind-key -T copy-mode-vi Enter send-keys -X copy-pipe-and-cancel 'xclip -selection clipboard'"
 
 # Inject @wname_style into status bar window tabs (must be after TPM/powerkit)
 # Makes agent state colors visible in the status bar, not just choose-tree
 run-shell '~/dotfiles/scripts/tmux/inject-wname-style.sh'
 
 # Colored choose-tree session/window picker (must be after TPM)
 # Tree labels (session/window names, markers) use pane fg — set via select-pane -P
 # pane-mode-changed hook resets pane style when exiting choose-tree
 # Agent colors set by watcher via @wname_style: running=green, idle=yellow, stuck=red
 set-hook -g pane-mode-changed[100] 'if -F "#{pane_in_mode}" "" "select-pane -P default"'
 bind s select-pane -P 'fg=#9ece6a' \; choose-tree -Zw -F '#{?pane_format,#[fg=#7dcfff]#{pane_current_command} #[fg=#565f89](#{pane_current_path}),#{?window_format,#{@wname_style}#{window_name}#{?window_active, *,},#[fg=#565f89]#{session_windows} windows#{?session_attached, #[fg=#e0af68](attached),}}}'
 bind w select-pane -P 'fg=#9ece6a' \; choose-tree -Zw -F '#{?pane_format,#[fg=#7dcfff]#{pane_current_command} #[fg=#565f89](#{pane_current_path}),#{?window_format,#{@wname_style}#{window_name}#{?window_active, *,},#[fg=#565f89]#{session_windows} windows#{?session_attached, #[fg=#e0af68](attached),}}}'
 
 # Context-aware r key: recall when in Claude, config reload otherwise
 # Must be after TPM to override tmux-powerkit's reload binding
 bind r if-shell "ps -o args= -t #{pane_tty} 2>/dev/null | grep -q 'claude'" \
     "confirm-before -p 'Kill Claude pane and run recall? (y/n)' 'respawn-pane -k \"/opt/homebrew/bin/recall\"'" \
     "source-file ~/.tmux.conf \\; display '#[fg=#a6e3a1]󰑓 Config Reloaded#[default]'"

parts.text: [Image 1] The colours don't seem to have changed

**Inspecting theme issues**

I need to address the issue where the user’s image colors are still bright green and blue. It seems like the tmux Powerkit theme might not be re-rendered due to cached settings or that the custom theme isn't loading properly. I'll check the plugin theme loader and see if the key names or color variants are generated correctly from the base. Maybe the existing tmux options are cached and not updating? I’ll look closely at the status-left and right options.

The screenshot still shows the old PowerKit-rendered colors, so the config reload accepted but the rendered status format is probably cached or being overridden after the theme file is sourced. I’ll inspect the live tmux options and PowerKit theme resolution rather than guessing.

input.command: tmux show -gqv @powerkit_custom_theme_path; tmux show -gqv @powerkit_separator_style; tmux show -gqv @powerkit_edge_separator_style; tmux show -gqv status-left; tmux show -gqv status-right; tmux show -gwv window-status-current-format; tmux show -gwv window-status-format

#!/usr/bin/env bash
# Helper script to reload theme after selection

POWERKIT_ROOT="${POWERKIT_ROOT:-$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")/../.." && pwd)}"

# Delete all theme caches (files)
rm -f ~/.cache/tmux-powerkit/data/theme_colors__* 2>/dev/null || true
rm -f ~/.cache/tmux-powerkit/data/current_theme 2>/dev/null || true

# Bootstrap minimal to get functions
. "${POWERKIT_ROOT}/src/core/bootstrap.sh"
powerkit_bootstrap_minimal

# CRITICAL: Clear in-memory global variables that cause fast-path to skip reload
unset _CURRENT_THEME
unset _CURRENT_VARIANT
# shellcheck disable=SC2034  # Variables used by bootstrap/theme system
declare -gA THEME_COLORS=()
# shellcheck disable=SC2034
declare -gA _COLOR_VARIANTS=()

5: # Usage: powerkit-render [side]

12: export POWERKIT_ROOT
13: 
14: # =============================================================================
15: # Rendered Output Cache (uses cache.sh functions)
16: # =============================================================================
17: # Cache the final rendered output for instant return.
18: # This prevents tmux "not ready" messages on session start.
19: # Uses the standard cache system so cache_clear_all cleans these too.
20: 
21: # Source bootstrap (auto-loads core + utils with source_guard)
22: . "${POWERKIT_ROOT}/src/core/bootstrap.sh"
23: 
24: # Load theme and separator (for loading placeholder)

26: . "${POWERKIT_ROOT}/src/renderer/separator.sh"
27: . "${POWERKIT_ROOT}/src/renderer/color_resolver.sh"
28: 
29: # Cache key for rendered output (includes theme to invalidate on theme change)
30: _render_cache_key() {
31:     local side="${1:-right}"
32:     local theme variant
33:     theme=$(get_tmux_option "@powerkit_theme" "${POWERKIT_DEFAULT_THEME}")
34:     variant=$(get_tmux_option "@powerkit_theme_variant" "${POWERKIT_DEFAULT_THEME_VARIANT}")
35:     printf 'rendered_%s__%s__%s' "$side" "$theme" "$variant"
36: }
37: 
38: # TTL for render cache (matches status-interval)
39: _RENDER_CACHE_TTL=5
40: 
41: # Check if render is already in progress (uses cache for lock)
42: _is_render_in_progress() {
43:     # Lock is valid for 30 seconds max
44:     cache_valid "render_lock" 30
45: }
46: 
47: # Show loading placeholder with animated spinner
48: # Usage: _show_loading "side"
49: _show_loading() {
50:     local side="${1:-right}"
51:

54:         $'\Uee06'
55:         $'\Uee07'
56:         $'\Uee08'
57:         $'\Uee09'
58:         $'\Uee0a'
59:         $'\Uee0b'
60:     )
61: 
62:     # Pick frame based on current second (pseudo-animation, Bash 5.0+)
63:     local frame_idx=$(( EPOCHSECONDS % 6 ))
64:     local icon="${spinner[$frame_idx]}"
65: 
66:     # Get colors from theme
67:     local bg fg status_bg transparent
68:     bg=$(get_color "info-base")
69:     fg=$(get_color "info-base-darkest")
70:     transparent=$(get_tmux_option "@powerkit_transparent" "${POWERKIT_DEFAULT_TRANSPARENT}")
71: 
72:     # Use resolve_color for status_bg to respect transparent mode
73:     status_bg=$(resolve_color "statusbar-bg")
74: 
75:     local output=""
76: 
77:     # Entry separator based on side
78:     if [[ "$side" == "right" || "$side" == "center" ]]; then
79:         # Left-pointing (◀) entry separator
80:         local entry_sep
81:         entry_sep=$(_get_separator_glyph "$(get_edge_separator_style)" "left")
82:         output+="#[fg=${bg},bg=${status_bg}]${entry_sep}"
83:     fi
84: 
85:     # Content

87: 
88:     # Exit separator based on side
89:     if [[ "$side" == "left" || "$side" == "center" ]]; then
90:         # Right-pointing (▶) exit separator
91:         local exit_sep
92:         exit_sep=$(_get_separator_glyph "$(get_edge_separator_style)" "right")
93:         output+="#[fg=${bg},bg=${status_bg}]${exit_sep}"
94:     fi

That means the custom theme option is set but not being used by the loader in this render path, so I’m checking the loader contract and will fix the config to use the option it actually reads.

1: #!/usr/bin/env bash
2: # =============================================================================
3: # PowerKit Core: Theme Loader
4: # Description: Loads theme files and initializes color system
5: # =============================================================================
6: 
7: # Source guard
8: POWERKIT_ROOT="${POWERKIT_ROOT:-$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")/../.." && pwd)}"
9: . "${POWERKIT_ROOT}/src/core/guard.sh"
10: source_guard "theme_loader" && return 0
11: 
12: . "${POWERKIT_ROOT}/src/core/defaults.sh"
13: . "${POWERKIT_ROOT}/src/core/logger.sh"
14: . "${POWERKIT_ROOT}/src/core/options.sh"
15: . "${POWERKIT_ROOT}/src/core/cache.sh"
16: . "${POWERKIT_ROOT}/src/core/color_generator.sh"
17: 
18: # =============================================================================
19: # Configuration (using defaults from defaults.sh)
20: # =============================================================================
21: 
22: _THEMES_DIR="${POWERKIT_ROOT}/src/themes"
23: _THEME_CACHE_KEY="current_theme"
24: 
25: # Current loaded theme
26: declare -g _CURRENT_THEME=""
27: declare -g _CURRENT_VARIANT=""
28: 
29: # Theme colors (populated by theme file)
30: declare -gA THEME_COLORS=()
31:

33: # Internal Functions
34: # =============================================================================
35: 
36: # Expand tilde in path
37: _expand_path() {
38:     local path="$1"
39:     # Handle escaped tilde
40:     path="${path//\\~/~}"
41:     # Expand tilde to home directory
42:     if [[ "$path" == "~"* ]]; then
43:         path="${HOME}${path:1}"
44:     fi
45:     printf '%s' "$path"
46: }
47: 
48: # Load theme from cache (uses cache API)
49: _load_cached_theme() {
50:     local cached
51:     # TTL=31536000 (1 year) - effectively permanent
52:     cached=$(cache_get "$_THEME_CACHE_KEY" 31536000) || return 1
53:     [[ -z "$cached" ]] && return 1
54: 
55:     # Parse theme/variant format
56:     if [[ "$cached" == *"/"* ]]; then
57:         _CURRENT_THEME="${cached%/*}"
58:         _CURRENT_VARIANT="${cached#*/}"
59:         return 0
60:     fi
61: 
62:     return 1
63: }
64: 
65: # Save current theme to cache (uses cache API)
66: _save_theme_cache() {
67:     cache_set "$_THEME_CACHE_KEY" "${_CURRENT_THEME}/${_CURRENT_VARIANT}"
68: }
69: 
70: # Get theme file path
71: _get_theme_path() {
72:     local theme="$1"
73:     local variant="$2"
74:

76:         local custom_path
77:         custom_path=$(get_tmux_option "@powerkit_custom_theme_path" "${POWERKIT_DEFAULT_CUSTOM_THEME_PATH}")
78:         custom_path=$(_expand_path "$custom_path")
79:         printf '%s' "$custom_path"
80:     else
81:         printf '%s/%s/%s.sh' "$_THEMES_DIR" "$theme" "$variant"
82:     fi
83: }

99:     local variant="${2:-${POWERKIT_DEFAULT_THEME_VARIANT}}"
100:

103:         log_debug "theme_loader" "Using in-memory theme: $theme/$variant"

109:     local cached_colors

114:         deserialize_theme_colors "$cached_colors"

117:             _CURRENT_THEME="$theme"

21:

23: # Plugin declared options storage
24: # Format: _PLUGIN_OPTIONS[plugin] = "name\x1Ftype\x1Fdefault\x1Fdesc;name2\x1F..."
25: declare -gA _PLUGIN_OPTIONS=()
26:

28: # Delimiter for option fields (ASCII Unit Separator)

30: 
31: # Plugin options value cache

33:

35: _DEFAULT_PLUGIN_OPTIONS=(

37: )
38: 
39: # =============================================================================
40: # Tmux Options API
41: # =============================================================================
42: 
43: # Batch load all @powerkit_* options from tmux
44: # This reduces multiple tmux calls to a single one
45: _batch_load_tmux_options() {
46:     [[ "$_TMUX_OPTIONS_LOADED" -eq 1 ]] && return 0
47: 
48:     # Check if we're inside tmux
49:     if [[ -z "${TMUX:-}" ]]; then
50:         _TMUX_OPTIONS_LOADED=1
51:         return 0
52:     fi
53: 
54:     local output
55:     output=$(tmux show-options -g 2>/dev/null | grep '^@powerkit' || true)
56: 
57:     while IFS= read -r line; do
58:         [[ -z "$line" ]] && continue
59:         # Parse: @powerkit_option "value" or @powerkit_option value
60:         if [[ "$line" =~ ^(@powerkit[a-zA-Z0-9_]+)[[:space:]]+\"?([^\"]*)\"?$ ]]; then
61:             local key="${BASH_REMATCH[1]}"
62:             local value="${BASH_REMATCH[2]}"
63:             _TMUX_OPTIONS_CACHE["$key"]="$value"
64:         fi
65:     done <<< "$output"
66: 
67:     _TMUX_OPTIONS_LOADED=1
68: }
69: 
70: # Inject default options for a plugin (call at start of plugin_declare_options)
71: _inject_default_plugin_options() {
72:     if [[ -z "$_CURRENT_PLUGIN" ]]; then
73:         log_error "options" "inject_default_plugin_options called without plugin context"
74:         return 1
75:     fi
76:     local opt
77:     for opt in "${_DEFAULT_PLUGIN_OPTIONS[@]}"; do
78:         # Só injeta se ainda não existir

82:         if [[ ",${current_opts}," != *,${name}${_OPT_DELIM}* ]]; then

84:             _PLUGIN_OPTIONS["$_CURRENT_PLUGIN"]+="$opt"
85:         fi
86:     done
87: }
88:

90: # Usage: get_tmux_option "@powerkit_option" "default"
91: get_tmux_option() {
92:     local option="$1"
93:     local default="$2"
94: 
95:     _batch_load_tmux_options
96: 
97:     # Check cache first
98:     if [[ -n "${_TMUX_OPTIONS_CACHE[$option]+x}" ]]; then
99:         printf '%s' "${_TMUX_OPTIONS_CACHE[$option]}"
100:         return 0
101:     fi
102: 
103:     # Not in cache, try direct tmux call (for non-@powerkit options)

105:         local value
106:         value=$(tmux show-option -gqv "$option" 2>/dev/null || true)
107:         if [[ -n "$value" ]]; then
108:             _TMUX_OPTIONS_CACHE["$option"]="$value"
109:             printf '%s' "$value"
110:             return 0
111:         fi
112:     fi
113: 
114:     printf '%s' "$default"
115: }

122:

125:         _TMUX_OPTIONS_CACHE["$option"]="$value"
126:     fi
127: }

parts.text:
**Exploring TMUX patch options**

I'm considering a potential patch: after TPM, I could run a direct plugin reload using a tmux command. The plugin options loader seems broken when there’s no TMUX, but I think I can ensure its process sees TMUX by setting it to the current server. I need to figure out how to get a valid TMUX environment. Using `tmux display-message`, I can extract the socket path, server PID, and session ID. Then, I can construct the right format to reload the theme. Let's give it a try!

The live status proves the problem: PowerKit has rendered Catppuccin into `status-left` and window formats, and it also reset `status-position`/`status-interval` to plugin defaults during the external reload. I’m going to invoke the renderer with a real tmux client environment so it can read the `@powerkit_theme custom` options instead of falling back to defaults.

1: #!/usr/bin/env bash
2: # =============================================================================
3: # PowerKit Renderer: Main Orchestrator
4: # Description: Orchestrates the rendering of all tmux visual elements
5: # =============================================================================
6: # The renderer is the high-level orchestrator. It:
7: # - Configures global status bar settings
8: # - Delegates layout composition to compositor.sh
9: # - Configures non-status-bar elements (panes, messages, clock, modes)
10: #
11: # The renderer does NOT build status formats directly - that's the compositor's job.
12: # =============================================================================
13: 
14: # Source guard
15: POWERKIT_ROOT="${POWERKIT_ROOT:-$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")/../.." && pwd)}"
16: . "${POWERKIT_ROOT}/src/core/guard.sh"
17: source_guard "renderer_main" && return 0
18: 
19: . "${POWERKIT_ROOT}/src/core/logger.sh"
20: . "${POWERKIT_ROOT}/src/core/options.sh"
21: . "${POWERKIT_ROOT}/src/core/lifecycle.sh"
22: . "${POWERKIT_ROOT}/src/renderer/color_resolver.sh"
23: . "${POWERKIT_ROOT}/src/renderer/separator.sh"
24: . "${POWERKIT_ROOT}/src/renderer/styles.sh"
25: . "${POWERKIT_ROOT}/src/renderer/compositor.sh"
26: 
27: # =============================================================================
28: # Status Bar Configuration
29: # =============================================================================
30: 
31: # Configure global status bar settings
32: # These are settings that apply regardless of layout
33: configure_status_bar() {
34:     log_debug "renderer" "Configuring status bar"
35: 
36:     # Status bar position
37:     local position
38:     position=$(get_tmux_option "@powerkit_status_position" "${POWERKIT_DEFAULT_STATUS_POSITION}")
39:     tmux set-option -g status-position "$position"
40: 
41:     # Status bar style
42:     local status_style
43:     status_style=$(build_status_style)
44:     tmux set-option -g status-style "$status_style"
45: 
46:     # Refresh interval
47:     local interval
48:     interval=$(get_tmux_option "@powerkit_status_interval" "${POWERKIT_DEFAULT_STATUS_INTERVAL}")
49:     tmux set-option -g status-interval "$interval"
50: 
51:     # Status bar lengths - ensure enough space for plugins and session
52:     # These are critical for proper display of all elements
53:     tmux set-option -g status-left-length "${POWERKIT_DEFAULT_STATUS_LEFT_LENGTH}"
54:     tmux set-option -g status-right-length "${POWERKIT_DEFAULT_STATUS_RIGHT_LENGTH}"
55: 
56:     log_debug "renderer" "Status bar configured"
57: }
58:

60: # Pane Configuration
61: # =============================================================================
62:

73:

76:     log_debug "renderer" "Configuring messages"
77: 
78:     # Message style

80:     msg_style=$(build_message_style)

82:

86:     tmux set-option -g message-command-style "$cmd_style"
87: 
88:     log_debug "renderer" "Messages configured"
89: }
90: 
91: # =============================================================================
92: # Clock Configuration
93: # =============================================================================
94: 
95: # Configure clock mode
96: configure_clock() {
97:     log_debug "renderer" "Configuring clock"
98: 
99:     local clock_color
100:     clock_color=$(build_clock_format)
101:     tmux set-option -g clock-mode-colour "$clock_color"
102: 
103:     local clock_style
104:     clock_style=$(get_tmux_option "@powerkit_clock_style" "${POWERKIT_DEFAULT_CLOCK_STYLE}")
105:     tmux set-option -g clock-mode-style "$clock_style"
106: 
107:     log_debug "renderer" "Clock configured"
108: }
109: 
110: # =============================================================================
111: # Mode Configuration
112: # =============================================================================
113: 
114: # Configure copy mode and other modes
115: configure_modes() {
116:     log_debug "renderer" "Configuring modes"
117: 
118:     # Mode style (copy mode highlight)
119:     local mode_style
120:     mode_style=$(build_mode_style)
121:     tmux set-option -g mode-style "$mode_style"
122: 
123:     log_debug "renderer" "Modes configured"
124: }
125: 
126: # =============================================================================
127: # Popup and Menu Configuration
128: # =============================================================================
129:

143: 
144:     # Popup border lines

146:     popup_border_lines=$(get_tmux_option "@powerkit_popup_border_lines" "${POWERKIT_DEFAULT_POPUP_BORDER_LINES}")
147:     tmux set-option -g popup-border-lines "$popup_border_lines" 2>/dev/null || true
148: 
149:     # Menu style
150:     local menu_style
151:     menu_style=$(build_menu_style)
152:     tmux set-option -g menu-style "$menu_style"
153: 
154:     # Menu selected style
155:     local menu_selected_style
156:     menu_selected_style=$(build_menu_selected_style)
157:     tmux set-option -g menu-selected-style "$menu_selected_style"
158: 
159:     # Menu border style
160:     local menu_border_style
161:     menu_border_style=$(build_menu_border_style)
162:     tmux set-option -g menu-border-style "$menu_border_style"
163: 
164:     # Menu border lines
165:     local menu_border_lines
166:     menu_border_lines=$(get_tmux_option "@powerkit_menu_border_lines" "${POWERKIT_DEFAULT_MENU_BORDER_LINES}")
167:     tmux set-option -g menu-border-lines "$menu_border_lines" 2>/dev/null || true
168: 
169:     log_debug "renderer" "Popups and menus configured"
170: }
171: 
172: # =============================================================================
173: # Full Render
174: # =============================================================================
175: 
176: # Run full render - applies all configurations
177: render_all() {
178:     log_info "renderer" "Starting full render"
179: 
180:     # Global status bar settings
181:     configure_status_bar
182: 
183:     # Layout composition (handles status-left, status-right, status-format, windows)
184:     compose_layout
185: 
186:     # Non-status-bar elements
187:     configure_panes
188:     configure_messages
189:     configure_clock
190:     configure_modes
191:     configure_popups_and_menus
192: 
193:     log_info "renderer" "Full render complete"
194: }
195: 
196: # Render only status bar (for updates)
197: render_status() {
198:     log_debug "renderer" "Rendering status bar"
199: 
200:     compose_layout
201: 
202:     log_debug "renderer" "Status bar rendered"
203: }
204: 
205: # Render with theme reload
206: render_with_theme() {
207:     log_info "renderer" "Rendering with theme reload"
208: 
209:     # Reload theme
210:     reload_theme
211: 
212:     # Render all
213:     render_all
214: 
215:     log_info "renderer" "Render with theme complete"
216: }
217: 
218: # =============================================================================
219: # Refresh Functions
220: # =============================================================================
221: 
222: # Refresh status bar (minimal update)
223: refresh_status() {
224:     tmux refresh-client -S 2>/dev/null || true
225: }
226: 
227: # Force full refresh
228: refresh_all() {
229:     render_all
230:     refresh_status
231: }
232: 
233: # =============================================================================
234: # Entry Points
235: # =============================================================================
236: 
237: # Initialize and render
238: init_renderer() {
239:     log_info "renderer" "Initializing renderer"
240: 
241:     # Make sure theme is loaded
242:     is_theme_loaded || load_powerkit_theme
243: 
244:     # Run full render
245:     render_all
246: 
247:     log_info "renderer" "Renderer initialized"
248: }
249: 
250: # Called by tmux-powerkit.tmux
251: run_powerkit() {
252:     init_renderer
253: }

# The renderer is the high-level orchestrator. It:
# - Configures global status bar settings
# - Delegates layout composition to compositor.sh
# - Configures non-status-bar elements (panes, messages, clock, modes)
#
# The renderer does NOT build status formats directly - that's the compositor's job.
# =============================================================================

# Source guard
POWERKIT_ROOT="${POWERKIT_ROOT:-$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")/../.." && pwd)}"
. "${POWERKIT_ROOT}/src/core/guard.sh"
source_guard "renderer_main" && return 0

. "${POWERKIT_ROOT}/src/core/logger.sh"
. "${POWERKIT_ROOT}/src/core/options.sh"

**Assessing status formatting**

I'm thinking about status formatting and the need for colors, especially if I should inspect it further. It seems like the status-left/right should be left blank for centered alignment. I’m also wondering about the use of the @_powerkit_left_edge; is it stale? I guess I need to figure out if color specifications in the status-format need customization. Maybe I’ll grep status-format colors to see what’s going on.

parts.text:
**Evaluating formatting options**

I’m looking at the formatting options and trying to figure out if underscores could serve as separators since it seems like using "none" would leave it empty. I think the Windows format uses underscores, maybe as placeholders. It might relate to how glyphs are rendered. I need to ensure the status on the right is cached properly. I wonder if I should run the powerkit-render in a TMUX environment for testing. Let's see how it behaves.

parts.text: The durable fix is to make `.tmux.conf` tell PowerKit its own `@powerkit_status_position`/`@powerkit_status_interval`, then add a post-TPM re-render that constructs a valid `TMUX` environment so PowerKit reads the configured custom theme instead of falling back. I’m adding that now.

input.patchText:
*** Begin Patch
*** Update File: /Users/shahe.islam/dotfiles/.tmux.conf
@@
 # Position status bar at bottom (user preference)
 set -g status-position bottom
+set -g @powerkit_status_position "bottom"
+set -g @powerkit_status_interval 15
@@
 # Override powerkit's default 5s status-interval (must be after TPM)
 set -g status-interval 15
+
+# PowerKit's renderer only batch-loads @powerkit_* options when TMUX is set.
+# External `tmux source-file` reloads can otherwise render the default theme.
+run-shell -b 'TMUX_SOCKET=$(tmux display-message -p "#{socket_path}" 2>/dev/null); TMUX_PID=$(tmux display-message -p "#{pid}" 2>/dev/null); TMUX_SESSION=$(tmux display-message -p "#{session_id}" 2>/dev/null); TMUX="$TMUX_SOCKET,$TMUX_PID,$TMUX_SESSION" POWERKIT_ROOT="$HOME/.tmux/plugins/tmux-powerkit" bash "$HOME/.tmux/plugins/tmux-powerkit/src/helpers/reload_theme.sh" >/dev/null 2>&1 || true'
*** End Patch

M .tmux.conf

Index: /Users/shahe.islam/dotfiles/.tmux.conf
===================================================================
--- /Users/shahe.islam/dotfiles/.tmux.conf
+++ /Users/shahe.islam/dotfiles/.tmux.conf
@@ -280,8 +280,10 @@
 set -g @powerkit_plugins 'git,datetime'
 
 # Position status bar at bottom (user preference)
 set -g status-position bottom
+set -g @powerkit_status_position "bottom"
+set -g @powerkit_status_interval 15
 
 # Theme configuration - Custom Tokyo Night Storm (editable)
 # To revert to built-in: set theme "tokyo-night" and variant "storm"
 # Edit colors in: scripts/tmux/tokyo-night-custom.sh
@@ -427,8 +429,12 @@
 
 # Override powerkit's default 5s status-interval (must be after TPM)
 set -g status-interval 15
 
+# PowerKit's renderer only batch-loads @powerkit_* options when TMUX is set.
+# External `tmux source-file` reloads can otherwise render the default theme.
+run-shell -b 'TMUX_SOCKET=$(tmux display-message -p "#{socket_path}" 2>/dev/null); TMUX_PID=$(tmux display-message -p "#{pid}" 2>/dev/null); TMUX_SESSION=$(tmux display-message -p "#{session_id}" 2>/dev/null); TMUX="$TMUX_SOCKET,$TMUX_PID,$TMUX_SESSION" POWERKIT_ROOT="$HOME/.tmux/plugins/tmux-powerkit" bash "$HOME/.tmux/plugins/tmux-powerkit/src/helpers/reload_theme.sh" >/dev/null 2>&1 || true'
+
 # Fix nested fish: reset default-command to empty after TPM loads.
 # tmux-sensible (loaded by TPM) overrides empty default-command with
 # "reattach-to-user-namespace -l $SHELL". By setting it to fish BEFORE TPM,
 # sensible sees non-empty value and leaves it alone. Now we reset to empty

files.patch:
Index: /Users/shahe.islam/dotfiles/.tmux.conf
===================================================================
--- /Users/shahe.islam/dotfiles/.tmux.conf
+++ /Users/shahe.islam/dotfiles/.tmux.conf
@@ -280,8 +280,10 @@
 set -g @powerkit_plugins 'git,datetime'
 
 # Position status bar at bottom (user preference)
 set -g status-position bottom
+set -g @powerkit_status_position "bottom"
+set -g @powerkit_status_interval 15
 
 # Theme configuration - Custom Tokyo Night Storm (editable)
 # To revert to built-in: set theme "tokyo-night" and variant "storm"
 # Edit colors in: scripts/tmux/tokyo-night-custom.sh
@@ -427,8 +429,12 @@
 
 # Override powerkit's default 5s status-interval (must be after TPM)
 set -g status-interval 15
 
+# PowerKit's renderer only batch-loads @powerkit_* options when TMUX is set.
+# External `tmux source-file` reloads can otherwise render the default theme.
+run-shell -b 'TMUX_SOCKET=$(tmux display-message -p "#{socket_path}" 2>/dev/null); TMUX_PID=$(tmux display-message -p "#{pid}" 2>/dev/null); TMUX_SESSION=$(tmux display-message -p "#{session_id}" 2>/dev/null); TMUX="$TMUX_SOCKET,$TMUX_PID,$TMUX_SESSION" POWERKIT_ROOT="$HOME/.tmux/plugins/tmux-powerkit" bash "$HOME/.tmux/plugins/tmux-powerkit/src/helpers/reload_theme.sh" >/dev/null 2>&1 || true'
+
 # Fix nested fish: reset default-command to empty after TPM loads.
 # tmux-sensible (loaded by TPM) overrides empty default-command with
 # "reattach-to-user-namespace -l $SHELL". By setting it to fish BEFORE TPM,
 # sensible sees non-empty value and leaves it alone. Now we reset to empty

 # Health states (variants auto-generated)

state.title: Reviews final muted tmux diff

content.summary: User asked: "Can we modify out tmux status bar so the colours are different and the edges aren't curved?" Work was done in `/Users/shahe.islam/dotfiles` on tmux config. Relevant files inspected: `.tmux.conf` and `scripts/tmux/tokyo-night-custom.sh`. Findings: tmux status bar is powered by `fabioluciano/tmux-powerkit`; custom theme path is `scripts/tmux/tokyo-night-custom.sh`; separator option was originally `set -g @powerkit_separator_style "rounded"`; PowerKit supports separator styles `normal`, `rounded`, `slant`, `slantup`, `trapezoid`, `flame`, `pixel`, `honeycomb`, `none`; `normal` gives Powerline arrow/diamond-like transitions, while `none` removes separator glyphs. Initial edit changed `.tmux.conf` to `normal` separators and made colors brighter (purple/teal). User clarified they did not want diamond edges and wanted darker/less bright colors. Superseding edit changed `.tmux.conf` to `set -g @powerkit_separator_style "none"`, `set -g @powerkit_edge_separator_style "none"`, and `set -g @powerkit_initial_separator_style "none"`, and changed fallback active window style to `fg=#c0caf5,bg=#2f3549,bold`. `scripts/tmux/tokyo-night-custom.sh` was changed to a muted rectangular Tokyo Night palette: `statusbar-bg=#16161e`, `statusbar-fg=#a9b1d6`, `session-bg=#24283b`, `session-fg=#a9b1d6`, `session-prefix-bg=#3b4261`, `session-copy-bg=#29394f`, `window-active-base=#2f3549`, `window-inactive-base=#1f2335`, `pane-border-active=#3b4261`, `pane-border-inactive=#1f2335`, `ok-base=#24283b`, `good-base=#31483f`, `info-base=#29394f`, `warning-base=#4a3f2d`, `error-base=#4a2f36`, `disabled-base=#3b4261`, muted message/popup/menu colors. A Bash associative-array issue was corrected by quoting keys like `THEME_COLORS["statusbar-bg"]` instead of unquoted/hyphenated syntax that had been formatted incorrectly. Validation run: `bash -n scripts/tmux/tokyo-night-custom.sh`, a runtime source test checking specific keys, and `tmux source-file "$HOME/.tmux.conf"`, all succeeded at that point. This earlier work was later found insufficient because PowerKit cached rendered status formats and/or fell back to built-in colors during external reloads.

parts.text: ▣ DCP | -31.7K removed, +4.4K summary — Compression #1

Fixed the live tmux reload issue.

What changed:
- PowerKit now gets `@powerkit_status_position "bottom"` and `@powerkit_status_interval 15`, so it does not reset those during render.
- Added a post-TPM `run-shell` re-render that constructs a valid `TMUX` env, forcing PowerKit to load the custom muted theme instead of falling back to default Catppuccin colors.
- Verified external reload with `tmux source-file "$HOME/.tmux.conf"` now renders muted custom colors like `#24283b`, `#2f3549`, `#29394f`, not the old bright green/blue.

The status bar should now update to the darker rectangular look after reload.

modified:   scripts/tmux/tokyo-night-custom.sh

Do we still need this?
I’ll inspect the current diff and live tmux dependency path to answer whether the theme file is still necessary, without changing anything.

state.title: Checks live PowerKit theme usage

parts.text:
Yes, if you want the darker colors.

`.tmux.conf` still points PowerKit at `scripts/tmux/tokyo-night-custom.sh`:

`@powerkit_theme custom`
`@powerkit_custom_theme_path "$HOME/dotfiles/scripts/tmux/tokyo-night-custom.sh"`

The live tmux format is using values from that file, for example `#24283b`, `#29394f`, `#2f3549`, and `#a9b1d6`.

If we drop the change to `scripts/tmux/tokyo-night-custom.sh`, you keep the square/no-separator edges from `.tmux.conf`, but colors revert to the previous brighter palette like green session and blue active window.
